html:
[629]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 彼所长惟火器 - Their strength lies solely in firearms; emphasizes a specific advantage.
  • 机发即燃 - When the mechanism is triggered, it ignites; describes the automatic ignition of firearms.
  • 不劳点焠 - No need for manual ignition; highlights the convenience of the technology.
  • 尺寸小物，力侔巨炮 - A small object, yet its power equals that of a large cannon; compares the effectiveness of the weapon to a much larger one.
  • 攻战之理皆谬 - All principles of attack and defense are flawed; indicates a critique of their military strategy.
  • 足蹑高低，不便疾行 - Their feet are not suited for quick movement; suggests physical limitations.
  • 多被杀伤，卧不能起 - They are often wounded and cannot get up; describes the vulnerability of the soldiers.
  • 将卒前取其首，辄为鸟炮所中 - When soldiers try to take their heads, they are hit by birdshot; illustrates a specific tactic and its counter.
  • 两胫间皆缚小炮，以膝对人，其炮自发 - Between their shins, they bind small cannons, aimed at people, which fire automatically; details a unique defensive mechanism.
  • 至死不僵 - Even when dead, they do not lie still; emphasizes their determination or the effectiveness of their weapons.
  • 凡所居处，下必藏火药 - In all their dwellings, below there must be hidden gunpowder; describes a strategic storage method.
  • 事急辄发其机，屋与人皆为飞灰 - In times of urgency, they trigger the mechanism, and both house and people turn to ashes; illustrates a devastating self-destruct mechanism.
  • 舟底亦然，急则自毁 - The bottom of their ships is the same, in urgency, they self-destruct; extends the self-destruct strategy to naval vessels.
  • 帆樯之巧，终不示人 - The ingenuity of their sails and masts is never shown to others; suggests secrecy in their technology.
  • 诸国罕闻效其制者 - Few countries have heard of or imitated their methods; indicates the uniqueness and rarity of their technology.
  • 红毛船 - Red-haired ship; likely refers to a foreign vessel, possibly European.
  • 遭风误过半线洋 - Encountered a storm and mistakenly passed the halfway line of the sea; describes a navigational error.
  • 遇浅船胶 - Encountered a shallow ship that stuck; indicates a physical obstacle.
  • 以布帆围蔽其舟，即于舟中另造小船 - They surrounded their ship with cloth sails and built a new small boat inside; describes a resourceful solution.
  • 郑氏视为釜鱼，方集战舰攻之 - The Zheng family viewed them as fish in a pot, gathering warships to attack them; illustrates a strategic advantage.
  • 悉登小船，扬帆而去 - They all boarded the small boat and sailed away; describes a successful escape.
  • 良久，机发，大船自毁 - After a while, the mechanism triggered, and the large ship self-destructed; shows the final act of self-destruction.
  • 人服其机警 - People admired their quick thinking; praising their strategic acumen.
  • 舟中百物俱备，造作小船需用物料，不假外求 - Everything needed for building a small boat was available on board, without needing to seek external supplies; highlighting their preparedness.
  • 叹不可及 - One cannot help but admire their ingenuity; expressing admiration for their resourcefulness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 彼所长惟火器 - Refers to the technological advantage of firearms in military conflicts.
  • 攻战之理皆谬 - Critiques the military strategies of the time, suggesting they were flawed.
  • 红毛船 - Likely refers to European ships, indicating the presence of foreign influences in Chinese maritime history.
  • 郑氏 - Refers to the Zheng family, a prominent maritime family in Chinese history, known for their naval prowess.
  • 帆樯之巧，终不示人 - Reflects the secrecy and ingenuity of Chinese naval technology during this period.
  • 诸国罕闻效其制者 - Highlights the uniqueness of Chinese naval technology, suggesting it was not widely known or imitated.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This long sequence describes the advantages and limitations of their firearms, requiring careful parsing to maintain clarity.
  • This series of clauses describes the physical and tactical disadvantages of their soldiers.
  • This clause details a unique defensive mechanism and its effectiveness.
  • This clause describes a self-destruct mechanism and its purpose.
  • This clause extends the self-destruct strategy to naval vessels and explains its secrecy.
  • This clause describes a navigational error and the resourceful solution.
  • This clause illustrates a strategic advantage and a successful escape.
  • This clause describes the final act of self-destruction and the admiration it garnered.
  • This clause highlights their preparedness and the admiration for their resourcefulness.

Proper Nouns

  • 红毛船 - Refers to a foreign vessel, likely European.
  • 郑氏 - Refers to the Zheng family, a prominent maritime family in Chinese history.

[630]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里乘子曰 - A phrase indicating that the following text is a statement or opinion attributed to someone named '里乘子.'
  • 曩游镇海 - A phrase meaning 'formerly traveled to Zhenhai,' indicating a past experience or observation.
  • 海舶之大者，其高如山 - A descriptive phrase comparing large ships to mountains in height, emphasizing their size.
  • 若以轧船环而攻之，彼虽高大，必不能逞 - A conditional statement outlining a military strategy and its expected outcome.
  • 方今寰海镜清，梯航靖谧 - A phrase describing the current state of the sea and distant regions as peaceful.
  • 固无论矣 - A concluding phrase that suggests the current situation is not a concern.
  • 然未雨绸缪，《诗》有明训 - A phrase introducing the idea of preparation and citing a well-known idiom.
  • 鄙意海疆各要隘，不妨多制轧船 - A phrase presenting the author's suggestion for improving coastal defenses.
  • 无事以之巡哨，有事即以之制敌 - A conditional statement outlining the dual use of the ships for patrolling and combat.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 里乘子 - A name that appears to be a pseudonym or a specific individual, but without additional context, it is difficult to provide more information.
  • 镇海 - A historical place name, likely referring to a coastal area or port city in China.
  • 海舶 - Refers to large ships used for maritime trade or military purposes.
  • 轧船 - A type of ship, possibly a specific design or class of vessel used in the context of the text.
  • 寰海 - Refers to the entire sea, often used in a metaphorical sense to describe the vastness of the ocean.
  • 梯航 - Refers to ships traveling to distant places, often used in the context of maritime exploration or trade.
  • 未雨绸缪 - A well-known idiom that emphasizes the importance of preparation and foresight.
  • 海疆 - Refers to coastal defenses or maritime borders, often used in the context of national security and military strategy.
  • 要隘 - Refers to strategic points or key locations, often used in the context of military or defensive planning.

Proper Nouns

  • 里乘子 - The name of the speaker or author.
  • 镇海 - A historical place name, likely referring to a coastal area or port city.
  • 海舶 - Refers to large ships.
  • 轧船 - A type of ship.
  • 寰海 - Refers to the entire sea.
  • 梯航 - Refers to ships traveling to distant places.
  • 未雨绸缪 - A well-known idiom.
  • 海疆 - Refers to coastal defenses.
  • 要隘 - Refers to strategic points.
  • 灵利安稳 - A descriptive phrase.
  • 寻常炮船 - Refers to ordinary warships.

[631]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 西洋國 - Refers to a country in the Western Ocean, likely a European nation, but the term is vague and lacks specific historical context.
  • 拗目隆准 - Describes a physical appearance, possibly referring to a distinct facial feature, but the exact meaning is unclear.
  • 紅毛 - A colloquial term for Europeans, often used in Chinese historical texts to describe foreigners with red hair or fair skin.
  • 心計 - Means 'cunning' or 'strategic thinking,' but the exact nuance in this context is unclear.
  • 堅忍之志 - Means 'determined and resilient will,' but the exact emphasis is unclear.
  • 析理務極精微 - Means 'analyzing principles to the utmost subtlety,' but the exact focus is unclear.
  • 象緯 - Refers to celestial phenomena or the study of astronomy.
  • 歷數不迨 - Means 'astronomy is not inferior,' but the exact comparison is unclear.
  • 器用小物 - Refers to small tools or devices, but the exact scope is unclear.
  • 造化之秘 - Means 'the secrets of nature' or 'the mysteries of creation.'
  • 後天地而不朽 - Means 'to outlast the heavens and earth,' a hyperbolic expression of immortality.
  • 七克 - Refers to a specific text or work, likely a religious or moral tract, but the exact content is unclear.
  • 孝悌慈讓 - Refers to Confucian virtues: filial piety, fraternal respect, kindness, and yielding.
  • 天主教之邪說 - Means 'heretical teachings of the Christian faith,' but the exact nature of the heresy is unclear.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西洋國 - Likely refers to a European country, but the specific nation is not identified.
  • 明季 - Refers to the late Ming dynasty (16th-17th century), a period of political and social upheaval in China.
  • 六書之學 - Refers to the study of the six principles of Chinese writing (pictographs, simple ideograms, compound ideograms, phonetic compounds, derivative cognates, and loan characters).
  • 利馬窦 - Refers to Matteo Ricci, a Jesuit missionary who played a significant role in introducing Western knowledge to China.
  • 天主教 - Refers to Christianity, specifically the Catholic Church, which was introduced to China during the Ming and Qing dynasties.

Proper Nouns

  • 西洋國 - A general term for a Western country.
  • 明季 - The late Ming dynasty.
  • 六書之學 - The study of Chinese writing principles.
  • 利馬窦 - Matteo Ricci, a Jesuit missionary.
  • 七克 - A specific religious or moral tract by Ricci.
  • 天主教 - Christianity, specifically the Catholic Church.

[632]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 中国人士被惑，多皈其教者 - Classical expression indicating that many Chinese people were influenced and converted to the religion.
  • 凡各省郡、县、卫、所，皆有天主堂，扃闭甚密，外人曾不得窥见所有 - Descriptive phrase indicating that in every province, prefecture, county, garrison, and station, there were Catholic churches that were very tightly sealed, and outsiders could never see what was inside.
  • 不耕不织，所用自饶，皆以诱人入教为务，谓之“化人” - Phrase indicating that the members of the religion did not engage in farming or weaving but had everything they needed, and their main purpose was to attract people to join the religion, which was called 'converting people.'
  • 彼国多产白金 - Statement indicating that the country (likely referring to Portugal or another European nation) produced a lot of silver.
  • 自明时已窃处粤之香山澳，虽纳贡赋，而丑类实据我边陲矣 - Phrase indicating that since the Ming dynasty, they had secretly established themselves in the Macau area of Guangdong, although they paid tribute, they were actually occupying our border regions.
  • 又岁运白金巨万至香山澳，转送各省郡邑天主堂，资其所用 - Phrase indicating that they annually transported millions of taels of silver to Macau, which was then sent to Catholic churches in various provinces, counties, and towns to support their activities.
  • 京师天主堂，屋宇宏丽，垣墙周复 - Descriptive phrase indicating that the Catholic church in the capital was grand and surrounded by walls.
  • 又制为风琴、自鸣钟、刻漏、浑天仪诸器，皆神镂鬼斧，巧夺天工，为费不可量 - Phrase indicating that they also made various instruments such as organs, self-striking clocks, water clocks, and celestial globes, all of which were ingeniously crafted, surpassing human skill, and the cost was immeasurable.
  • 穷年积岁，制造不辍，不藉中国一钱 - Phrase indicating that they continued to manufacture these items year after year without interruption, without relying on a single coin from China.
  • 余谓红毛密迩西洋，自是同类，英圭黎、咬留巴皆西海小国，宜为并兼，不足深怪 - Phrase indicating that I believe the Portuguese are close to the Western countries and are of the same kind, and countries like England, Guinea, and Brazil are small Western nations, so it is not surprising that they are annexed.
  • 独怪吕宋在东海外，远过中国万里，亦为所踞，此其心宁有厌足乎 - Phrase indicating that it is strange that Luzon (the Philippines) is far away from China, more than ten thousand miles east, and is also occupied by them, does their ambition know no bounds?
  • 闽人多有逐利吕宋者，谓红毛政令，一如西洋之法 - Phrase indicating that many people from Fujian went to Luzon for profit, and they said that the Portuguese government's laws were the same as those of the Western countries.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中国人士 - Refers to Chinese people.
  • 各省郡、县、卫、所 - Refers to the administrative divisions of the Ming dynasty.
  • 天主堂 - Refers to Catholic churches.
  • 扃闭甚密 - Refers to the tight sealing of the churches.
  • 不耕不织 - Refers to the members of the religion not engaging in manual labor.
  • 所用自饶 - Refers to having everything they needed.
  • 化人 - Refers to converting people to the religion.
  • 彼国 - Refers to the country (likely Portugal or another European nation).
  • 白金 - Refers to silver.
  • 明时 - Refers to the Ming dynasty.
  • 粤之香山澳 - Refers to the Macau area of Guangdong.
  • 丑类 - Refers to the Portuguese or another European nation.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital (likely Beijing).
  • 风琴、自鸣钟、刻漏、浑天仪 - Refers to various instruments.
  • 红毛 - Refers to the Portuguese or another European nation.
  • 英圭黎、咬留巴 - Refers to England, Guinea, and Brazil.
  • 西海 - Refers to the Western countries.
  • 吕宋 - Refers to the Philippines.
  • 闽人 - Refers to people from Fujian.
  • 红毛政令 - Refers to the government's laws of the Portuguese or another European nation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence is elliptical but means that many Chinese people were influenced and converted to the religion.
  • The sentence is a descriptive list of the presence and secrecy of Catholic churches.
  • The sentence describes the lifestyle and purpose of the members of the religion.
  • The sentence is a simple statement about the production of silver in the country.
  • The sentence describes the historical context of the Portuguese establishing themselves in Macau.
  • The sentence describes the annual transportation of silver to support the activities of the Catholic churches.
  • The sentence describes the grandeur of the Catholic church in the capital.
  • The sentence describes the craftsmanship of the instruments made by the Catholic churches.
  • The sentence describes the continuous manufacturing activities of the Catholic churches without relying on Chinese resources.
  • The sentence expresses the author's view on the relationship between the Portuguese and other Western nations.
  • The sentence expresses the author's surprise at the Portuguese occupation of the Philippines.
  • The sentence describes the activities of people from Fujian in Luzon and their observations about the Portuguese government.

Proper Nouns

  • 中国 - China.
  • 各省郡、县、卫、所 - Administrative divisions of the Ming dynasty.
  • 天主堂 - Catholic churches.
  • 彼国 - The country (likely Portugal or another European nation).
  • 白金 - Silver.
  • 明时 - Ming dynasty.
  • 粤之香山澳 - Macau area of Guangdong.
  • 丑类 - The Portuguese or another European nation.
  • 京师 - Capital (likely Beijing).
  • 红毛 - The Portuguese or another European nation.
  • 英圭黎、咬留巴 - England, Guinea, and Brazil.
  • 西海 - Western countries.
  • 吕宋 - Philippines.
  • 闽人 - People from Fujian.
  • 红毛政令 - Government's laws of the Portuguese or another European nation.

[633]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 分吕宋地为二十四郡 - Literal phrase meaning 'to divide Luzon into twenty-four prefectures,' indicating a territorial division.
  • 禁民不得昼作，必使昼寝夜兴 - Command structure that prohibits daytime labor and mandates daytime sleep and nighttime activity, reflecting a strict schedule.
  • 又寝室不容闭门，夫妇共寝榻上，逻者时时绕榻前侦视 - Describes a surveillance system where doors cannot be closed, couples must share a bed, and guards constantly monitor around the bed.
  • 有女及笄，父母不得主婚配，必侯巴黎按选 - Refers to a custom where unmarried girls cannot be married by their parents but must wait for a selection by 'Paris.'
  • 称僧曰巴黎 - An unusual term where '僧' (monk) is referred to as '巴黎' (Paris), which is likely a mistranscription or a cultural reference.
  • 其稍有姿者，率为巴黎所留，色衰放归，始令择配 - Describes a practice where attractive women are kept by 'Paris' until they lose their beauty, after which they are released to choose a partner.
  • 父母死，人子不得殓埋，巴黎假度亡之说，舁置万人坑中；积久坑溢，扬灰弃之 - Describes a cruel practice where children cannot bury their dead parents, and bodies are placed in mass graves, eventually being cremated and discarded.
  • 民人有资，岁与中分，四岁之后，十不存一矣 - Indicates a system where resources are divided annually, leading to severe depletion over time.
  • 其禁昼作者，防民之叛也 - Explains the daytime prohibition as a measure to prevent rebellion.
  • 岁中分其资者，务贫其民，使不得为所欲为也 - Describes the annual division of resources as a strategy to impoverish the people and prevent them from acting freely.
  • 死者不令瘗埋，恐由川毓灵，欲无复生英杰与争国也 - Explains the prohibition on burying the dead as a way to prevent the rise of new leaders who might challenge the ruling power.
  • 用意设想，皆在人情之外 - Concludes that the measures are beyond normal human considerations.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吕宋 - Refers to Luzon, an island in the Philippines.
  • 巴黎 - Likely a mistranscription or a cultural reference, possibly referring to a group or authority figure.
  • 万人坑 - Refers to mass graves, often associated with historical atrocities.
  • 中分 - Means 'divided equally,' indicating a fair distribution of resources.

Proper Nouns

  • 吕宋 - Luzon, an island in the Philippines.
  • 巴黎 - Likely a mistranscription or a cultural reference, possibly referring to a group or authority figure.
  • 万人坑 - Mass graves, often associated with historical atrocities.
  • 中分 - Divided equally, indicating a fair distribution of resources.

[634]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秦皇焚书坑儒 - Refers to the historical event where Qin Shi Huang burned books and buried Confucian scholars alive to suppress dissent and unify thought.
  • 禁偶语 - Means 'prohibiting private conversations,' a measure to control the spread of ideas.
  • 徙豪杰以弱天下之民 - Means 'moving the powerful to weaken the people,' a strategy to reduce the influence of local elites.
  • 欲令子孙自一世、二世以至万世 - Means 'intending for his descendants to rule for generations, even to the ten-thousandth generation.'
  • 其祸尚忍言哉 - A rhetorical question meaning 'could one bear to speak of the disaster?'
  • 人情莫不恋故乡而安本俗 - Means 'human nature is to love one's hometown and be content with one's native customs.'
  • 若弃父母、捐家室 - Means 'if one abandons parents and family.'
  • 而反为利己者 - Means 'and instead seeks personal gain.'
  • 计中国郡、邑、卫、所 - Refers to the administrative divisions of China, including prefectures, counties, garrisons, and guard posts.
  • 天主堂 - Refers to Catholic churches.
  • 丑类 - A derogatory term for foreigners, particularly Westerners.
  • 脱有不信余言者 - Means 'if there are those who do not believe my words.'
  • 试问日本何以禁绝丑类，不令蹈其境乎 - Means 'ask why Japan bans foreigners from entering its territory.'
  • 巴黎选女之事 - Refers to the incident in Paris where women were selected for marriage, likely a reference to the practice of choosing brides for Westerners in China.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦皇 - Refers to Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China, known for his authoritarian rule and centralization of power.
  • 焚书坑儒 - A notorious event in Chinese history where Qin Shi Huang ordered the burning of books and the execution of Confucian scholars to suppress dissent.
  • 禁偶语、徙豪杰以弱天下之民 - These were political measures taken by Qin Shi Huang to control the population and reduce the power of local elites.
  • 天主堂 - Refers to Catholic churches, which were often seen as centers of foreign influence in China.
  • 丑类 - A derogatory term used in Chinese historical texts to refer to foreigners, particularly Westerners, often with negative connotations.
  • 巴黎选女之事 - Likely refers to the practice of selecting brides for Westerners in China, particularly in the context of the late Qing dynasty and early Republican era.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦皇 - Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.
  • 天主堂 - Catholic churches.
  • 丑类 - A derogatory term for foreigners, particularly Westerners.
  • 日本 - Japan.
  • 巴黎 - Paris, France.

[635]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里乘子曰 - Formal opening that introduces the narrator, who is identified as '里乘子.'
  • 凡入天主教者 - Refers to individuals who have joined the Catholic Church.
  • 至天主堂拜其酋长 - Literally means 'go to the church to worship its chief,' where '酋长' (chief) refers to the priest or the leader of the church.
  • 给银三十两 - Means 'given thirty taels of silver,' where '银' (silver) refers to money, and '两' (taels) is a unit of weight used for currency.
  • 趣吞之 - Means 'quickly swallow it,' where '趣' (quickly) emphasizes the urgency.
  • 心日昏迷 - Means 'the heart gradually becomes confused,' where '日' (gradually) indicates a progressive change.
  • 视祖孙父子皆为平等 - Means 'to see ancestors, grandchildren, and parents as equal,' reflecting a loss of hierarchical respect.
  • 除天主外，凡天地神祗祖先，一概不祀 - Means 'except for the Lord, all deities, gods, and ancestors are no longer worshipped,' indicating a shift in religious practice.
  • 天理人道，绝灭殆尽 - Means 'the principles of heaven and human conduct are nearly extinguished,' suggesting a moral and ethical decline.
  • 有诸生某甲 - Refers to a scholar named '甲' (A).
  • 素恶此教 - Means 'has always disliked this religion.'
  • 以贫难自存 - Means 'due to poverty, he struggles to survive.'
  • 欲骗其银 - Means 'wants to trick the money.'
  • 又虑其丸作祟 - Means 'but fears the pill will cause harm.'
  • 商之妻 - Means 'discuss with his wife.'
  • 使预浓煎下药 - Means 'have her prepare a strong decoction to counteract the pill.'
  • 既拜其酋长，吞丸携银归 - Means 'after worshipping the chief, swallowing the pill, and taking the money home.'
  • 急索药服之 - Means 'quickly take the medicine.'
  • 果将丸泄出 - Means 'the pill was indeed expelled.'
  • 爰取刀斧劈之，不能开 - Means 'he then took an axe to chop it, but could not break it open.'
  • 怒甚，投诸炉火，亦竟不灰 - Means 'angrily, he threw it into the fire, but it did not burn.'
  • 方惊诧间，忽闻剥喙声 - Means 'just as he was surprised, he suddenly heard a pecking sound.'
  • 启户视之，乃其酋长使人来 - Means 'he opened the door and saw that it was a messenger from the chief.'
  • 责甲悖教 - Means 'the messenger accused A of violating the teachings.'
  • 并索取银丸 - Means 'and demanded the money and the pill back.'
  • 词色甚厉 - Means 'his words and demeanor were very severe.'
  • 甲强与辨 - Means 'A tried to argue back.'
  • 其人笑曰 - Means 'the messenger laughed and said.'
  • 君休饶舌，顷丸神已归 - Means 'you stop talking, the spirit of the pill has returned.'
  • 诉汝投诸炉火，可速取还，免罹后患也 - Means 'it has reported you for throwing it into the fire, so quickly retrieve it, or else you will suffer consequences.'
  • 甲无如何，遂并归之 - Means 'A had no choice but to return both the money and the pill.'
  • 初，甲颇能文 - Means 'originally, A was quite skilled in writing.'
  • 自后忽忽若失，文思顿踬矣 - Means 'afterward, he became distracted and lost his ability to write.'
  • 又言凡入天主教者，其家人有病，必报知酋长为之医治 - Means 'it is also said that anyone who joins the Catholic Church must inform the chief if a family member is ill, and the chief will treat them.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天主教 - Refers to Catholicism, a major Christian denomination.
  • 天主堂 - Refers to a Catholic church.
  • 酋长 - Refers to a priest or the leader of the church.
  • 天主 - Refers to God in Catholicism.
  • 天地神祗 - Refers to deities and gods in traditional Chinese religion.
  • 祖先 - Refers to ancestors, who are revered in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 诸生 - Refers to scholars or students, often used in the context of the imperial examination system.
  • 剥喙声 - Refers to a pecking sound, which could be a metaphor for a supernatural event or a symbolic representation of divine intervention.
  • 丸神 - Refers to the spirit or essence of the pill, suggesting a supernatural element.
  • 炉火 - Refers to a fire in a furnace, often used for burning or melting objects.
  • 文思顿踬 - Refers to a sudden loss of writing ability, which could be a metaphor for a spiritual or mental decline.
  • 家人有病 - Refers to family members being ill, a common concern in traditional Chinese society.
  • 酋长为之医治 - Refers to the priest or church leader providing medical treatment, which was not a typical role for religious figures in traditional Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 里乘子 - The narrator of the story.
  • 宁波 - A city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 天主教 - Catholicism.
  • 天主堂 - Catholic church.
  • 酋长 - Priest or church leader.
  • 天主 - God in Catholicism.
  • 天地神祗 - Deities and gods in traditional Chinese religion.
  • 祖先 - Ancestors.
  • 诸生 - Scholars or students.
  • 丸神 - Spirit or essence of the pill.
  • 炉火 - Fire in a furnace.
  • 剥喙声 - Pecking sound.
  • 文思顿踬 - Sudden loss of writing ability.
  • 家人 - Family members.
  • 酋长为之医治 - Priest or church leader providing medical treatment.

[636]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不能起 - Ambiguous; could mean 'cannot be revived' or 'cannot be raised up,' referring to someone near death.
  • 濒危时 - Means 'when in peril of death' or 'at the time of imminent danger.'
  • 酋长先入其房 - Literally 'the chieftain enters his room first,' suggesting a ritual or ceremonial act.
  • 闭门密幕窗棂 - Means 'closes the door and screens the window lattice,' indicating a secretive or sacred space.
  • 为之诵咒 - Means 'recites incantations for him/her,' referring to a religious or magical ritual.
  • 度其生天 - Means 'judges if he/she is destined for heaven,' suggesting a belief in an afterlife.
  • 俟气绝乃开门 - Means 'waits until he/she breathes his/her last before opening the door,' indicating a specific ritual sequence.
  • 令所亲入房举哀殡殓 - Means 'allows relatives to enter the room to mourn and prepare for burial,' describing the aftermath of the ritual.
  • 有某一家 - Means 'there was a certain family,' introducing a specific example.
  • 素奉天主教甚虔 - Means 'has always devoutly followed the Catholic faith,' indicating a strong religious commitment.
  • 其母病革 - Means 'his mother was gravely ill,' describing the critical condition of the mother.
  • 酋长如例为之闭门诵咒 - Means 'the chieftain, as usual, closed the door and recited incantations for her,' suggesting a routine ritual.
  • 有戚某，不信其说 - Means 'there was a certain relative who did not believe in this,' introducing a skeptic.
  • 以房故有楼，悄伏其上 - Means 'because the house had a second floor, he quietly hid on it,' describing the skeptic's action.
  • 钻隙下窥 - Means 'peered through a gap,' indicating the skeptic's method of observation.
  • 见酋长扶病者坐床上 - Means 'saw the chieftain helping the sick person sit on the bed,' describing the ritual act.
  • 以被靠其后，怀取一物塞其口 - Means 'placed a pillow behind her and took something from his chest to plug her mouth,' describing a disturbing act.
  • 又出一针，长三寸许，刺其两目 - Means 'then took out a needle, about three inches long, and pricked her eyes,' describing another disturbing act.
  • 血涔涔出 - Means 'blood trickled out,' describing the result of the pricking.
  • 又取一小铜圆盒承其血 - Means 'then took a small copper round box to catch the blood,' describing the collection of blood.
  • 复袒其胸，以针刺其心 - Means 'then exposed her chest and pricked her heart with a needle,' describing a further disturbing act.
  • 又取一小铜方盒承之 - Means 'then took a small copper square box to catch it,' describing the collection of blood from the heart.
  • 既拭去血迹，出膏药贴其两目并当胸小孔 - Means 'after wiping away the blood, he took ointment and applied it to her eyes and the small hole on her chest,' describing the final steps of the ritual.
  • 口中喃喃诵咒，不知云何 - Means 'muttered incantations under his breath, though it is unknown what they were,' describing the incantations.
  • 举家罗拜伏地称谢讫 - Means 'the whole family gathered to prostrate themselves and thank him,' describing the family's reaction.
  • 然后入房举哀 - Means 'then entered the room to mourn,' describing the family's mourning.
  • 戚某自上窥之最确 - Means 'the relative who had been watching from above confirmed it,' describing the skeptic's confirmation.
  • 后以语人，闻者无不匿笑 - Means 'later told others, and those who heard it could not help but secretly laugh,' describing the reaction of others.
  • 然则由前而论，吞丸之作法甚诡 - Means 'Thus, considering the previous account, the method of swallowing a pill is very strange,' summarizing the skeptic's view.
  • 由后而论，取血之居心更毒 - Means 'Considering the later account, the intent to take blood is even more sinister,' further elaborating the skeptic's view.
  • 彼给银三十两者，固为欲买两盒血计耳 - Means 'He gave thirty-two taels of silver, clearly intending to buy the two boxes of blood,' explaining the motivation.
  • 吾窃怪匹夫匹妇纷纷入教，一身受其害而不知 - Means 'I secretly wonder why ordinary men and women join the religion so eagerly, unaware of the harm they are suffering,' expressing the author's skepticism.
  • 一家受其害而亦不知，趋之若狂，执迷不悟，亦不重可悲哉！ - Means 'A family suffers harm and is also unaware, rushing into it crazily, stubbornly refusing to see the truth, which is truly pitiful!' expressing the author's lament.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 酋长 - Refers to a tribal leader or chieftain, often associated with traditional or indigenous practices.
  • 天主教 - Refers to Catholicism, a major branch of Christianity.
  • 天 - In this context, refers to heaven or the afterlife, a common concept in many religions.
  • 病革 - Refers to a critical illness, a common term in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 闭门密幕窗棂 - Describes a ritual space that is closed off and made private, often used in religious or magical rituals.
  • 诵咒 - Refers to reciting incantations, a common practice in many religions and belief systems.
  • 生天 - Refers to being destined for heaven, a concept in many religions.
  • 气绝 - Refers to the cessation of breathing, a common term in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 举哀殡殓 - Refers to mourning and preparing for burial, a common funeral ritual.

[637]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宇内形势 - Refers to the overall layout and structure of the world, both physical and conceptual.
  • 天宇外涵 - A poetic way of saying the universe encompasses everything, with the sky surrounding the earth.
  • 大地虚悬于中 - Describes the earth as floating in the center of the universe, a concept derived from ancient Chinese cosmology.
  • 古以卵为喻 - Ancient people used the metaphor of an egg to describe the universe, which is close to the idea of the earth being at the center.
  • 海水附隶于地 - Seawater is attached to the earth, emphasizing the interconnectedness of land and sea.
  • 包山川原隰 - Includes mountains, rivers, plains, and wetlands, indicating the comprehensive nature of the earth's features.
  • 四夷八荒 - Refers to the four border regions and the eight distant corners of the world, representing the outer limits of the known world.
  • 各占一区 - Each region occupies its own area, suggesting a structured division of the world.
  • 如盆盎中贮水石然 - Like water and stones stored in a basin or vase, a metaphor for the containment and organization of the world.
  • 驺衍论天地之大 - Zhuang Yan, a philosopher, discussed the vastness of the heavens and the earth.
  • 如中国赤县神州 - Like China, the central land of the world, a term used to refer to the central and most important part of the world.
  • 禹之所序者 - What Yu the Great organized, referring to the legendary emperor who established the Xia Dynasty and organized the world.
  • 八十一分之一 - One out of eighty-one parts, a specific fraction used to describe the size of China relative to the whole world.
  • 外此尚有九州 - Outside of this, there are nine more regions, indicating the existence of additional areas beyond the central region.
  • 凡一州 - Each of these regions.
  • 各有裨海环之 - Each has a smaller sea surrounding it, emphasizing the isolation of each region.
  • 人民禽兽 - People and animals, indicating the inhabitants of these regions.
  • 莫能相通 - Cannot communicate or interact, highlighting the separation between regions.
  • 如此者九 - Nine such regions, reinforcing the idea of nine separate areas.
  • 环其外 - Surrounds the outside, indicating the boundary of the world.
  • 吾人所居所谓中华大国者 - The great nation we inhabit, referring to China.
  • 以四天之下 - Under the four directions, emphasizing the central position of China.
  • 惟中华恰居天地之中耳 - Only China is precisely at the center of the heavens and the earth.
  • 不知天地之体既皆圆矣 - Not knowing that the bodies of heaven and earth are both round, indicating a misunderstanding of the shape of the universe.
  • 人处宇内 - People living in the universe, emphasizing the human perspective.
  • 头戴天而足履地 - With their heads touching the sky and feet touching the earth, a metaphor for the human position in the universe.
  • 何莫非中 - How can any place not be the center, questioning the notion of a fixed center.
  • 若必求天地之中 - If one must seek the center of heaven and earth, a conditional statement.
  • 惟北极天枢之下 - Only under the North Pole's celestial pivot, a specific point in the sky.
  • 此处如轮之毂 - This place is like the hub of a wheel, a metaphor for the central point.
  • 如磨之脐 - Like the navel of a millstone, another metaphor for the central point.
  • 如人身之心 - Like the heart of a person, a metaphor for the central and vital point.
  • 庶几足以当之 - Perhaps it can be considered the center, a cautious conclusion.
  • 然天枢之下 - However, under the celestial pivot.
  • 实在朔漠以北 - Is actually north of the northern deserts, a specific geographical location.
  • 去禹迹甚远 - Far from the traces of Yu, indicating the distance from the legendary emperor's influence.
  • 中国一区 - One region of China, emphasizing the specific area.
  • 道里虽广 - The distance is broad, indicating the large size of China.
  • 若以天枢揆之 - If measured by the celestial pivot, a method of measurement.
  • 其实偏在东南 - It is actually in the southeast, indicating the actual position of China.
  • 而东南半壁 - And the southeastern half, emphasizing the specific part of China.
  • 又皆海也 - Is also sea, indicating that the southeastern part is mostly covered by water.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宇内 - Refers to the entire world, a term used in ancient Chinese cosmology.
  • 天宇 - The sky and universe, a common term in classical Chinese literature.
  • 大地 - The earth, a fundamental element in Chinese cosmology.
  • 卵 - Egg, a metaphor used to describe the universe, reflecting ancient Chinese beliefs.
  • 海水 - Seawater, a natural element that is part of the earth's surface.
  • 山川原隰 - Mountains, rivers, plains, and wetlands, representing the diverse features of the earth.
  • 四夷八荒 - The four border regions and the eight distant corners of the world, a term used to describe the outer limits of the known world.
  • 驺衍 - Zhuang Yan, a philosopher from the Warring States period, known for his cosmological theories.
  • 赤县神州 - The central land of the world, a term used to refer to China.
  • 禹 - Yu the Great, a legendary emperor who organized the world and established the Xia Dynasty.
  • 九州 - Nine regions, a division of the world in ancient Chinese cosmology.
  • 裨海 - Smaller seas, a term used to describe the bodies of water surrounding each region.
  • 中华 - China, the great nation, a term used to refer to the central and most important part of the world.
  • 四天之下 - Under the four directions, emphasizing the central position of China.
  • 北极天枢 - The North Pole's celestial pivot, a specific point in the sky used to determine the center of the universe.
  • 朔漠 - Northern deserts, a geographical term used to describe the northern part of China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自辽阳至此 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'from Liao Yang to here,' indicating a geographical starting point.
  • 中国东面已尽 - Means 'the eastern part of China has reached its limit,' marking the eastern boundary.
  • 琼厓之间 - Refers to the Qiong崖 (琼崖) region, which is a specific area in Hainan Island.
  • 实为东南隅 - Means 'it is indeed the southeastern corner,' emphasizing the geographical position.
  • 缘海转西 - Means 'turning west along the coast,' describing a geographical movement.
  • 中国之南面矣 - Means 'it becomes the southern part of China,' indicating a shift in direction.
  • 尽粤东地而西为粤西 - Means 'reaching the western part of Guangdong Province,' describing a geographical progression.
  • 更西为贵州 - Means 'further west is Guizhou Province,' continuing the geographical description.
  • 又更西为云南省 - Means 'even further west is Yunnan Province,' extending the geographical range.
  • 云南者，中国西南隅也 - Means 'Yunnan is the southwestern corner of China,' emphasizing its location.
  • 然云南尽处，不尽于海而尽于山 - Means 'however, the end of Yunnan does not reach the sea but reaches the mountains,' describing the geographical boundary.
  • 崇山复嶂 - Refers to 'towering mountains and layered cliffs,' describing the terrain.
  • 倮偶、苗夷所居 - Means 'the homes of the Lolo and Miao people,' indicating the inhabitants.
  • 又有缅甸国 - Means 'there is also the country of Burma,' introducing another nation.
  • 皆瘴疠害人 - Means 'all are plagued by malaria and other diseases,' describing the health risks.
  • 人不能入 - Means 'people cannot enter,' indicating the difficulty of access.
  • 而海港亦仅止于粤西之某郡 - Means 'but the seaports also only reach a certain county in western Guangdong,' describing the extent of maritime access.
  • 未尝达云南也 - Means 'they have never reached Yunnan,' emphasizing the limitation.
  • 自琼、厓间渡海港而南 - Means 'crossing the sea port south of Qiong and Ya,' describing a journey.
  • 水程七更 - Means 'a seven-day sea voyage,' indicating the duration of travel.
  • 抵东京 - Means 'reaching Tokyo,' indicating the destination.
  • 国名，本交阯也 - Means 'the name of the country, originally Jiaozhi,' providing historical context.
  • 明时黎氏为外家所据，遂另为一国 - Means 'during the Ming Dynasty, the Li family was taken over by an external power, thus becoming a separate country,' explaining the political situation.
  • 东京又渡海港，十二更抵安南 - Means 'from Tokyo, crossing another sea port, it takes twelve days to reach Annam,' continuing the journey.
  • 即古交阯国 - Means 'which is the ancient Jiaozhi country,' providing historical context.
  • 东京、安南两海港，自港口横渡虽甚广，渐西渐隘，而海亦止，盖海之支汊也 - Means 'the sea ports of Tokyo and Annam, although wide when crossing from the port, gradually narrow to the west, and the sea also ends, as it is a branch of the sea,' describing the geography of the sea ports.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 辽阳 - A historical city in northeastern China, known for its strategic importance.
  • 中国 - Refers to the central plains of China, the core area of Chinese civilization.
  • 贵州 - A province in southwestern China, known for its mountainous terrain.
  • 云南 - A province in southwestern China, known for its diverse ethnic groups and mountainous terrain.
  • 倮偶、苗夷 - Refers to the Lolo and Miao ethnic groups, indigenous peoples of southern China.
  • 缅甸国 - The country of Burma (now Myanmar), located to the west of Yunnan.
  • 东京 - In this context, refers to a place in Southeast Asia, not the modern-day Tokyo in Japan.
  • 交阯 - An ancient Chinese term for the region that includes modern-day northern Vietnam.
  • 明时 - Refers to the Ming Dynasty (1368-1644), a period of Chinese history.
  • 黎氏 - Refers to the Li family, a ruling dynasty in the region.
  • 外家 - Refers to an external power, likely a neighboring state or dynasty.
  • 安南 - An ancient name for Vietnam, used during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 古交阯国 - Refers to the ancient Jiaozhi kingdom, which corresponds to the region of modern-day northern Vietnam.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 實與 - Emphasizes a direct and continuous relationship.
  • 立銅柱，不以海為限，而以分茅嶺為限 - Describes the historical boundary established by the Han general using a physical landmark and a geographical feature.
  • 接壤可知 - Indicates a clear and recognized boundary.
  • 皆與中國連 - Suggests a continuous geographical and political connection to China.
  • 中國以其鄙遠，棄而不收 - Indicates a historical policy of neglect due to distance and remoteness.
  • 麻六甲為西面盡處 - Emphasizes Malacca's geographical position as the westernmost end.
  • 凡海舶由厦门直指南 - Describes the route of maritime travel from Xiamen directly south.
  • 水程七十更，安南七十二更，暹羅一百八十更 - Specifies the number of stages required to reach different destinations, emphasizing the distances involved.
  • 漸偏而西 - Describes the change in direction of the maritime route, gradually turning westward.
  • 歷六昆、大年，又轉北過柔佛 - Details the specific points along the route, passing through Six Khmer, Daen, and then turning north over Johor.
  • 始抵麻六甲，水程二百更 - Emphasizes the total distance of the journey to Malacca.
  • 至此，約已轉出云南、缅甸后矣 - Indicates the relative position of Malacca in relation to Yunnan and Myanmar.

  • 分茅嶺 - Refers to the Fenshuo Mountain, a historical boundary marker.
  • 柬浦寨 - Refers to Cambodia.
  • 暹罗 - Refers to Siam, the historical name for Thailand.
  • 六昆 - Refers to the Khmer Empire, specifically the region of Six Khmer.
  • 大年 - Refers to a historical kingdom in Southeast Asia, possibly the Srivijaya Empire.
  • 柔佛 - Refers to the historical state of Johor in Malaysia.
  • 麻六甲 - Refers to Malacca, a historically significant port city in Malaysia.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 久之，女择所爱者，乃与挽手，挽手者，以明私许之意也 - After some time, the girl chooses the one she loves and holds hands with him, holding hands being a sign of private consent.

  • 婚姻无媒妁 - This reflects a society where marriages are arranged without the involvement of formal matchmakers.
  • 女已长，父母使居别室中 - This indicates a practice of secluding young women in separate rooms, possibly for protection or moral reasons.
  • 少年求偶者，皆来吹鼻萧、弹口琴，得女子和之，即入与乱，乱毕自去 - This describes a courtship ritual involving music, which is a unique and informal method of mate selection.
  • 久之，女择所爱者，乃与挽手，挽手者，以明私许之意也 - This describes a private agreement between a young man and a young woman, symbolized by holding hands.
  • 明日，女告其父母，召挽手少年至，凿上腭门牙傍二齿授女，女亦凿二齿付男 - This describes a ritual of tooth exchange, which may have symbolic or binding significance in the marriage.
  • 期某日就妇室成婚，终身依妇以处 - This describes a traditional marriage arrangement where the man lives with the woman for the rest of his life.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蓋皆以門楣紹瓜瓞 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'generally speaking, they all pass on their lineage like melons and gourds,' indicating the continuity of family lines.
  • 父母不得有其子 - Means 'parents cannot claim their children,' suggesting that the children may belong to another lineage or be lost to the family.
  • 故一再世，而孫且不識其祖矣 - Means 'thus, after one or two generations, the grandchildren may not even know their grandparents,' emphasizing the loss of ancestral knowledge.
  • 番人皆無姓氏 - Means 'barbarians all lack surnames,' referring to non-Han ethnic groups that do not follow the Han naming conventions.
  • 番室仿龜殼為制 - Means 'the houses of barbarians are modeled after turtle shells,' describing the architectural style of the houses.
  • 茅檐深远垂地 - Means 'the thatched eaves extend far and hang low to the ground,' describing the structure of the roof.
  • 過土基方丈 - Means 'overhanging the earth base by a square zhang,' indicating the extent of the overhang.
  • 雨陽不得侵 - Means 'rain and sun cannot penetrate,' emphasizing the effectiveness of the design in protecting the interior.
  • 其下可舂、可炊，可坐、可臥 - Means 'below, one can pound grain, cook, sit, and lie down,' describing the multifunctional nature of the space.
  • 以貯笨車網罟農具 - Means 'to store carts, nets, and farming tools,' indicating the practical uses of the space.
  • 雞栖豚栅 - Means 'chickens roost and pigs are penned,' describing the living conditions of animals.
  • 無不宜 - Means 'nothing is inappropriate,' suggesting that everything fits well in the design.
  • 室前后各為牖 - Means 'windows are made at the front and back of the room,' describing the ventilation.
  • 緣梯而登 - Means 'climb up by ladder,' indicating how one accesses the upper part of the house.
  • 室中空無所有 - Means 'the room is empty of everything,' emphasizing the simplicity of the interior.
  • 視有幾犬，為置幾榻 - Means 'depending on how many dogs there are, several beds are placed,' describing the minimalistic approach to furniture.
  • 人惟藉鹿皮随处卧 - Means 'people only lie on deer skins wherever they are,' indicating the simplicity of bedding.
  • 夏并鹿皮去之，藉地而已 - Means 'in summer, the deer skins are removed, and one lies directly on the ground,' describing the seasonal adaptation.
  • 壁間懸葫蘆，大如斗 - Means 'hemp rope is hung on the wall, as large as a dou (a unit of measure),' describing the storage of supplies.
  • 旨蓄毯衣納其中 - Means 'preserved food and woolen clothes are stored inside,' indicating the contents of the storage.
  • 竹筒數規 - Means 'several bamboo tubes,' describing the containers.
  • 則新醅也 - Means 'these are new brews,' indicating the contents of the bamboo tubes.
  • 其釀法 - Means 'their method of brewing,' introducing the brewing process.
  • 聚男女老幼，共嚼米納筒中 - Means 'men, women, old and young gather together, chewing rice and putting it into the tubes,' describing the communal brewing process.
  • 數日成酒 - Means 'in several days, the wine is made,' indicating the duration of the brewing process.
  • 飲時，入清泉和之 - Means 'when drinking, clear water is added to it,' describing the dilution of the wine.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 門楣 - Refers to the lintel of a door, often associated with family lineage.

  • 番人 - Refers to non-Han ethnic groups, often used to describe people outside the mainstream Han culture.
  • 龜殼 - Symbolizes protection and longevity in Chinese culture.

  • 農具 - Refers to farming tools.
  • 鹿皮 - Refers to deer skins, often used as bedding.
  • 葫蘆 - Refers to gourds, often used as containers.
  • 斗 - A unit of measure, approximately 10 liters.
  • 旨蓄 - Refers to preserved food.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sentence sets up a contrast between the continuity of lineage and the loss of children to other lineages.
  • This clause follows logically from the previous one, emphasizing the loss of ancestral knowledge.
  • This clause provides a reason for the lack of surnames among barbarians.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 客至 - Common phrase meaning 'when guests arrive.'
  • 番妇倾筒中酒，先尝，然后进客 - Describes a custom where the hostess tastes the wine before serving it to the guests.
  • 客饮尽则喜，否则愠 - Indicates that the hostess is pleased if the guest finishes the wine, otherwise she becomes angry.
  • 为联袂之歌以侑觞 - Describes a custom where the hostess sings songs to accompany the drinking.
  • 不计止宿 - Means 'without considering where to stay.'
  • 食云则食，坐云则坐 - Indicates that the people eat and sit according to their whims.
  • 喜一笑，痛一颦 - Describes the people's emotional expressions, smiling when happy and frowning when in pain.
  • 终岁不知春夏，老死不知年岁 - Means that they live through the year without knowing the seasons and die without knowing their age.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 番妇 - Refers to foreign women, indicating a cultural context where the text is describing customs of a non-Chinese group.
  • 衣毯衣 - Refers to wearing clothing made of felt or wool, suggesting a nomadic or semi-nomadic lifestyle.
  • 联袂之歌 - Refers to a type of song performed by women, often associated with hospitality and entertainment.
  • 侑觞 - Refers to the act of singing or performing to accompany drinking, a common practice in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 海外皆称中国为大唐，称中国人为唐人 - Indicates that people in other countries refer to China as 'Great Tang' and Chinese people as 'Tang people,' reflecting the influence of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 饔飧不宿舂 - Refers to a custom of not preparing food the night before, indicating a nomadic lifestyle where food is prepared as needed.
  • 手抟食之 - Refers to eating by hand, suggesting a simple and direct way of consuming food.
  • 山中多糜鹿 - Refers to the abundance of deer in the mountains, indicating a natural environment rich in wildlife.
  • 肉之生熟不甚较，果腹而已 - Suggests that the people are not particular about the cooking of meat, as long as it fills their stomachs.
  • 不虑风雨，行不计止宿 - Indicates a nomadic lifestyle where the people do not worry about weather conditions or where to stay.
  • 食云则食，坐云则坐 - Describes a spontaneous and flexible way of living, where actions are taken according to immediate circumstances.
  • 喜一笑，痛一颦 - Describes a direct and unfiltered expression of emotions.
  • 终岁不知春夏，老死不知年岁 - Suggests a life lived in harmony with nature, without the need for precise timekeeping or seasonal awareness.

Proper Nouns

  • 大唐 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty, a period of great cultural and political influence in Chinese history.
  • 唐人 - Refers to Chinese people, particularly those from the Tang Dynasty.

[652]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 寒然后求之衣，饥然后求之食 - Phrase meaning 'when cold, one seeks clothing; when hungry, one seeks food.' Reflects a minimalist lifestyle where basic needs are met only when necessary.
  • 不预计也 - Phrase meaning 'without planning ahead,' indicating a lack of foresight or preparation.
  • 村落庐舍，各为向背 - Phrase describing village houses being built in various orientations, suggesting a lack of uniformity or central planning.
  • 无市肆贸易 - Phrase meaning 'no market trade,' indicating that there are no commercial activities or markets in the village.
  • 有金钱无所用 - Phrase meaning 'money has no use,' suggesting that the villagers do not engage in economic transactions that require money.
  • 不知蓄积 - Phrase meaning 'do not know how to accumulate,' indicating a lack of savings or long-term planning.
  • 惟知计食而耕 - Phrase meaning 'only know to plant according to what they eat,' suggesting a subsistence farming lifestyle where crops are grown based on immediate consumption needs.
  • 番人无男女皆嗜酒 - Phrase meaning 'all men and women of the tribe love drinking,' indicating a cultural preference for alcohol consumption.
  • 酒熟，各携所酿，聚男女酣饮，歌呼如沸，累三日夜不辍 - Phrase describing a scene where everyone brings their own wine, gathers together to drink, sing, and shout, continuing for three days and nights without stopping. Emphasizes the communal and festive nature of the drinking.
  • 屋必自构，衣需自织 - Phrases meaning 'houses must be built by oneself' and 'clothes must be woven by oneself,' indicating a self-sufficient lifestyle.
  • 耕田而后食，汲涧而后饮 - Phrases meaning 'eat after farming' and 'drink after fetching water from the stream,' emphasizing the direct relationship between labor and sustenance.
  • 绩麻为网，屈竹为弓，以猎以渔 - Phrases describing the process of making nets from hemp and bows from bamboo, used for hunting and fishing, highlighting the practical skills required for survival.
  • 盖毕世所需，罔非自为而后用之 - Phrase meaning 'all the necessities of life are made by oneself and used,' reinforcing the theme of self-sufficiency and self-reliance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 番人 - Refers to a tribe or ethnic group, likely indigenous people living in a remote area.
  • 曲蘖 - Refers to the process of making alcohol, specifically from fermented grains.
  • 村落庐舍 - Describes the simple and scattered nature of the village dwellings, typical of rural or tribal communities.
  • 市肆贸易 - Refers to market trade, which is absent in this community, indicating a lack of commercial activity.
  • 金钱 - Refers to money, which is not used in this community, suggesting a barter system or a lack of economic exchange.
  • 蓄积 - Refers to the act of saving or accumulating resources, which is not practiced in this community.
  • 计食而耕 - Describes a subsistence farming practice where crops are grown based on immediate consumption needs.
  • 番人无男女皆嗜酒 - Highlights a cultural practice where both men and women enjoy drinking, which is a significant social activity in this community.
  • 酒熟，各携所酿，聚男女酣饮，歌呼如沸，累三日夜不辍 - Describes a communal drinking festival, which is a common social event in many traditional societies.
  • 屋必自构，衣需自织 - Emphasizes the self-sufficient nature of the community, where all basic needs are met through personal effort.
  • 耕田而后食，汲涧而后饮 - Reflects the direct and immediate relationship between labor and sustenance, typical of a subsistence lifestyle.
  • 绩麻为网，屈竹为弓，以猎以渔 - Describes the practical skills and tools used for survival, such as weaving nets and making bows.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 寒然后求之衣，饥然后求之食 - Phrase is straightforward but can be read as a series of conditional statements, each describing a specific need and its corresponding action.
  • 村落庐舍，各为向背 - Phrase is a simple description but can be read as a contrast between the uniformity and lack of uniformity in village construction.
  • 无市肆贸易，有金钱无所用 - Phrase is a compound statement that links the absence of market trade to the lack of use for money.
  • 番人无男女皆嗜酒 - Phrase is a simple statement but can be read as a cultural observation about the drinking habits of the tribe.
  • 酒熟，各携所酿，聚男女酣饮，歌呼如沸，累三日夜不辍 - Phrase is a descriptive sequence that can be read as a series of actions leading to a communal drinking festival.
  • 屋必自构，衣需自织 - Phrases are simple statements but can be read as a contrast between the different aspects of self-sufficiency.
  • 耕田而后食，汲涧而后饮 - Phrases are simple statements but can be read as a contrast between the different aspects of labor and sustenance.
  • 绩麻为网，屈竹为弓，以猎以渔 - Phrase is a descriptive sequence that can be read as a series of actions leading to the creation of tools for survival.
  • 盖毕世所需，罔非自为而后用之 - Phrase is a general statement that summarizes the self-sufficient nature of the community.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 腰間一刃，行卧与俱 - Literal: 'a sword at the waist, carried everywhere.' Metaphorical: 'always with the person.'
  • 凡所成造，皆出于此 - Literal: 'everything created comes from this.' Metaphorical: 'the sword as the source of all creation.'
  • 惟陶冶不能自为 - Literal: 'only pottery making cannot be done by oneself.' Metaphorical: 'a limitation in self-sufficiency.'
  • 得铁，则取涧中两石夹捶之 - Literal: 'if iron is obtained, then take two stones from the stream and hammer it between them.' Metaphorical: 'a method of forging.'
  • 剖匏截竹，用代陶瓦 - Literal: 'cut gourds and chop bamboo to use as substitutes for pottery and tiles.' Metaphorical: 'resourcefulness.'
  • 挹酒浆，可似胹饼 - Literal: 'to pour wine and soup, can be used to cook food.' Metaphorical: 'the utility of the substitutes.'
  • 我有之，我饮食之 - Literal: 'I have it, I eat and drink from it.' Metaphorical: 'emphasizing personal use.'
  • 乡党亲戚，缓急有无不相通 - Literal: 'for neighbors, relatives, in times of need, there is no sharing of resources.' Metaphorical: 'lack of communal support.'
  • 邻人米烂仓红，饥者不之贷也 - Literal: 'neighbors' rice rots in the granary, the hungry do not borrow from them.' Metaphorical: 'further illustrating the lack of mutual aid.'
  • 社有小大，户口有众寡 - Literal: 'villages have large and small, households have many and few.' Metaphorical: 'describing the diversity of communities.'
  • 皆推一二人为土官 - Literal: 'they all elect one or two people as local officials.' Metaphorical: 'a form of local governance.'
  • 其居室、饮食、力作，皆与众等 - Literal: 'their living quarters, food, and labor are all equal to the common people.' Metaphorical: 'equality among officials and commoners.'
  • 无一毫加于众番 - Literal: 'not a single bit added to the common people.' Metaphorical: 'emphasizing the officials' modesty.'
  • 不似滇广土官，征赋税，操杀夺，拥兵自卫者 - Literal: 'unlike the local officials of Yunnan and Guangdong, who collect taxes, wield power, and maintain their own armies.' Metaphorical: 'contrasting with corrupt officials.'
  • 比其先不知有君长 - Literal: 'compared to their ancestors, they did not know of any king or leader.' Metaphorical: 'indicating a lack of centralized authority.'
  • 自红毛始据时，平地土番悉受约束，力役输赋，不敢违法 - Literal: 'since the time the Red-haired (Dutch) began to rule, the flatland tribes were all subject to their rules, performing labor and paying taxes, and dared not break the law.' Metaphorical: 'showing the impact of foreign rule.'
  • 杀人者剿灭无孑遗 - Literal: 'those who kill are exterminated without a single survivor.' Metaphorical: 'indicating strict punishment.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陶冶 - Refers to pottery making, a traditional craft.
  • 剖匏截竹 - Refers to cutting gourds and bamboo, common materials in traditional Chinese households.
  • 挹酒浆，可似胹饼 - Refers to the practical use of these materials in daily life.
  • 乡党亲戚 - Refers to neighbors and relatives, important social ties in traditional Chinese society.
  • 社有小大，户口有众寡 - Refers to the diversity of villages and households, reflecting the social structure of the time.
  • 土官 - Refers to local officials, a form of governance in traditional Chinese society.
  • 滇广土官 - Refers to local officials in Yunnan and Guangdong provinces, known for their corruption.
  • 红毛 - Refers to the Dutch, who had a presence in China during this period.
  • 平地土番 - Refers to the flatland tribes, indigenous peoples in the region.
  • 力役输赋 - Refers to labor and tax obligations, common under foreign rule.
  • 杀人者剿灭无孑遗 - Refers to the strict enforcement of law, particularly in dealing with violent offenders.

Proper Nouns

  • 红毛 - Refers to the Dutch.
  • 滇广 - Refers to the provinces of Yunnan and Guangdong.
  • 土官 - Refers to local officials.
  • 平地土番 - Refers to the flatland tribes.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鄭氏繼至 - Refers to the Zheng family's subsequent rule in Taiwan.
  • 立法尤嚴 - Enacted laws especially strictly, emphasizing the severity of their legal system.
  • 诛夷不遗赤子 - Executed and exiled without sparing even the youngest, highlighting the harshness of their punishments.
  • 并田畴庐舍废之 - Also abandoned their fields, houses, and shelters, indicating the destruction of property.
  • 非谋不轨也 - Not for plotting rebellion, clarifying that the killings were not politically motivated.
  • 曲蘖误之也 - Due to the intoxication caused by alcohol, explaining the cause of the violence.
  • 群饮之际 - During communal drinking sessions, setting the context for the violence.
  • 夸力争强 - Boasting and striving to outdo each other, describing the competitive nature of the gatherings.
  • 互不相下 - Refusing to yield to each other, indicating the intensity of the competition.
  • 白刃以陷其脰 - With swords piercing their throats, vividly describing the violence.
  • 间有平时睚眦 - Sometimes there were grudges from past grievances, explaining the underlying tensions.
  • 醉后修怨 - Settling old scores after drinking, indicating the escalation of conflicts.
  • 旦日酒醒 - The next day when they sobered up, showing the aftermath of the violence.
  • 曾不自知 - Without even realizing it, highlighting the unconscious nature of their actions.
  • 讨罪之师已蹑其门矣 - The army of retribution had already arrived at their door, indicating the swift response to the violence.
  • 至今大肚牛骂犬甲竹堑诸社 - Today, the Dadao, Niwamai, Qianjia, and Zhujian tribes, referring to specific indigenous groups.
  • 林莽荒秽 - Forests and jungles overgrown with weeds, describing the desolate state of the land.
  • 不见一人 - Not a single person is seen, emphasizing the complete abandonment of the area.
  • 诸番视此为戒 - The various tribes view this as a warning, indicating the impact on other indigenous groups.
  • 相率谓曰 - They all say, indicating a collective response.
  • 红毛强犯之无噍类 - The Red-haired (Dutch) invaders left no survivors, referring to the Dutch colonial rule.
  • 郑氏来，红毛畏之逃去 - When the Zheng clan came, the Red-haired fled in fear, showing the shift in power dynamics.
  • 今郑氏又为皇帝剿灭 - Now the Zheng clan is being eliminated by the emperor, indicating the current political situation.
  • 尽为臣虏 - All became subjects and captives, emphasizing the complete subjugation.
  • 皇帝真天威矣 - The emperor truly possesses the divine power, expressing awe and submission.
  • 故其人既愚又甚畏法 - Therefore, they are both foolish and very afraid of the law, summarizing the indigenous people's attitude.
  • 曩郑氏于诸番徭赋颇重 - Previously, the Zheng clan imposed heavy taxes and corvée labor on the tribes, indicating the economic burden.
  • 我朝因之 - Our dynasty continued this, showing the continuity of policies.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鄭氏 - Refers to the Zheng family, who ruled Taiwan in the late 17th century.
  • 红毛 - A term used to refer to the Dutch colonizers, derived from their red hair.
  • 大肚牛骂犬甲竹堑诸社 - Specific indigenous tribes in Taiwan, including Dadao, Niwamai, Qianjia, and Zhujian.
  • 皇帝 - Refers to the Qing Emperor, who conquered Taiwan and brought it under Qing rule.

Proper Nouns

  • 鄭氏 - The Zheng family.
  • 红毛 - The Dutch colonizers.
  • 大肚牛骂犬甲竹堑诸社 - Specific indigenous tribes in Taiwan.
  • 皇帝 - The Qing Emperor.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秋成输谷似易 - Literally means 'the autumn harvest of grain seems easy,' but it implies that while the harvest itself is straightforward, the subsequent process of paying taxes is more challenging.
  • 艰于输赋 - Means 'difficult in paying taxes,' highlighting the difficulty of fulfilling tax obligations.
  • 彼终世不知白镪为何物 - '彼' refers to the indigenous people, and '终世' means 'throughout their lives.' '白镪' is a term for silver or money, indicating that these people have never seen or used silver.
  • 安所得此以贡其上 - '安得' means 'how can they possibly get,' and '贡其上' means 'to offer to their superiors,' suggesting that the indigenous people cannot obtain silver to pay their taxes.
  • 仍沿包社之法 - '仍沿' means 'still follow,' and '包社之法' refers to an old system where wealthy individuals in the county or town would take on the responsibility of collecting and paying taxes on behalf of the community.
  • 社商又委通事夥长辈 - '委' means 'entrust,' and '通事夥长辈' refers to trusted and experienced individuals who are given the responsibility of managing the community's affairs.
  • 凡番人一粒一毫，皆有籍稽之 - '凡' means 'all,' '番人' refers to the indigenous people, and '籍稽' means 'recorded and verified,' indicating that every detail of the indigenous people's resources is meticulously recorded.
  • 射得麋鹿，尽取其肉为脯，并收其皮 - '尽取其肉为脯' means 'take all the meat and make it into jerky,' and '并收其皮' means 'and collect the hides,' showing the thorough exploitation of the hunted animals.
  • 日本人甚需鹿皮，有贾舶收买 - '甚需' means 'very much need,' and '贾舶' refers to merchant ships, indicating that Japanese merchants are in high demand for deer hides and actively purchase them.
  • 脯以鬻漳郡人 - '鬻' means 'sell,' and '漳郡人' refers to people from the region of Zhangzhou, suggesting that the jerky is sold to local residents.
  • 二者输赋有馀 - '二者' refers to the deer hides and jerky, and '输赋有馀' means 'more than enough to pay taxes,' indicating that the two products generate sufficient revenue.
  • 然此辈欺番人愚，剥削无厌 - '欺番人愚' means 'take advantage of the indigenous people's naivety,' and '剥削无厌' means 'exploit them without limit,' highlighting the exploitative nature of the '社商'.
  • 视所有不异己物 - '视所有不异己物' means 'regard everything as their own property,' indicating that the '社商' treat the resources of the indigenous people as if they were their own.
  • 平时事无巨细，悉呼番人男妇孩稚，供役其室无虚日 - '悉呼番人男妇孩稚' means 'call upon all the men, women, and children of the indigenous people,' and '供役其室无虚日' means 'to perform labor for them without a day off,' showing the constant and exhaustive labor demands placed on the indigenous people.
  • 且皆纳番妇为妻妾 - '纳番妇为妻妾' means 'take indigenous women as wives and concubines,' indicating the sexual exploitation of the indigenous women.
  • 有求必与，有过必挞 - '有求必与' means 'give in to any request,' and '有过必挞' means 'beat them for any mistake,' highlighting the abusive treatment of the indigenous people.
  • 而番人不甚怨之 - '不甚怨之' means 'do not greatly resent them,' suggesting that the indigenous people do not strongly resist or complain about their exploitation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 番人 - Refers to the indigenous people of the region, often used in historical texts to describe non-Han Chinese groups.
  • 白镪 - A term for silver or money, reflecting the use of silver as a form of currency in China during this period.
  • 包社之法 - An old system where wealthy individuals in the county or town would take on the responsibility of collecting and paying taxes on behalf of the community, reflecting the social and economic structures of the time.
  • 社商 - The name given to those who take on the role of collecting and managing communal taxes, indicating a specific social role in the community.
  • 通事夥长辈 - Trusted and experienced individuals who are given the responsibility of managing the community's affairs, reflecting the importance of trusted intermediaries in local governance.
  • 日本人 - Refers to Japanese merchants, indicating the presence of international trade and the demand for certain goods like deer hides.
  • 漳郡人 - People from the region of Zhangzhou, reflecting the local economic and social dynamics.
  • 剥削 - Exploitation, reflecting the economic and social inequalities of the time.
  • 番妇 - Indigenous women, reflecting the gender dynamics and social hierarchies of the period.

Proper Nouns

  • 番人 - The indigenous people of the region.
  • 白镪 - Silver or money.
  • 包社之法 - The old system of communal tax collection.
  • 社商 - The individuals who take on the role of collecting and managing communal taxes.
  • 通事夥长辈 - Trusted and experienced individuals who manage the community's affairs.
  • 日本人 - Japanese merchants.
  • 漳郡人 - People from the region of Zhangzhou.
  • 剥削 - Exploitation.
  • 番妇 - Indigenous women.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 苟能化以礼义，风以诗书 - Means 'if one can transform through ritual and righteousness, and cultivate through poetry and books.' This is a compound phrase that combines two methods of moral and cultural transformation.
  • 教以蓄有备无之道 - Means 'teach the way of preparing for what is present and ready for what is absent.' This is a metaphorical expression emphasizing foresight and preparation.
  • 制以衣服、饮食、冠婚、丧祭之礼 - Means 'regulate through the rites of clothing, food, marriage, and funeral rites.' This phrase lists specific aspects of daily life that are governed by ritual.
  • 使咸知爱亲敬长、尊君亲上 - Means 'to make everyone know how to love their parents, respect the elderly, honor the ruler, and be close to superiors.' This is a compound phrase that lists the moral virtues to be cultivated.
  • 启发乐生之心，潜削顽愍之性 - Means 'to inspire a joy of life, and subtly diminish the stubborn and pitiable nature.' This is a metaphorical expression combining positive and negative aspects of human nature.
  • 远则百年，近则三十年 - Means 'in the long term, it could take a hundred years; in the short term, it could take thirty years.' This is a compound phrase that indicates a range of time frames.
  • 将见风俗改观，率循礼教 - Means 'one will see a change in customs, and a general adherence to ritual and education.' This is a compound phrase that describes the expected outcome of the proposed transformation.
  • 宁与中国之民有以异乎 - Means 'how much different would they be from the people of China?' This is a rhetorical question that emphasizes the similarity between the target population and the Chinese people.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 礼义 - Refers to ritual and righteousness, key concepts in Confucian thought.
  • 诗书 - Refers to the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry) and *Shujing* (Book of Documents), two of the Five Classics.
  • 蓄有备无之道 - Refers to the Confucian ideal of being prepared for both present and future challenges.
  • 衣服、饮食、冠婚、丧祭之礼 - Refers to specific aspects of daily life that are governed by ritual, emphasizing the importance of ritual in social life.
  • 爱亲敬长、尊君亲上 - Refers to the Confucian virtues of filial piety, respect for elders, loyalty to the ruler, and closeness to superiors.
  • 乐生之心 - Refers to the Confucian ideal of finding joy in life.
  • 顽愍之性 - Refers to the negative aspects of human nature that need to be diminished.
  • 荆蛮 - Refers to the Jing and Man tribes, historically associated with the southern regions of China.
  • 断发文身 - Refers to the specific cultural practice of cutting hair and tattooing bodies, associated with the Jing and Man tribes.
  • 吴越 - Refers to the Wu and Yue regions, historically associated with the southern regions of China.
  • 人文渊薮 - Refers to a rich source of culture, emphasizing the transformation of a region.
  • 闽地 - Refers to the Min region, historically associated with the southern regions of China.
  • 叛服不常 - Refers to the political instability of the Min region.
  • 汉世 - Refers to the Han dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.
  • 道南先生 - Refers to a specific historical figure, likely a scholar or philosopher.
  • 宋理学 - Refers to Song Neo-Confucianism, a significant philosophical movement in Chinese history.
  • 大儒 - Refers to great scholars, emphasizing the rise of intellectual figures.
  • 南中 - Refers to the southern regions of China.
  • 台郡 - Refers to the administrative region of Taiwan.
  • 海外劳吏 - Refers to officials working in overseas territories, emphasizing the administrative status of Taiwan.
  • 每三岁迁擢 - Refers to the administrative practice of promoting officials every three years.

Proper Nouns

  • 礼 - Refers to ritual, a key concept in Confucian thought.
  • 义 - Refers to righteousness, a key concept in Confucian thought.
  • 诗 - Refers to the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), one of the Five Classics.
  • 书 - Refers to the *Shujing* (Book of Documents), one of the Five Classics.
  • 汉世 - Refers to the Han dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.
  • 道南先生 - Refers to a specific historical figure, likely a scholar or philosopher.
  • 宋理学 - Refers to Song Neo-Confucianism, a significant philosophical movement in Chinese history.
  • 大儒 - Refers to great scholars, emphasizing the rise of intellectual figures.
  • 南中 - Refers to the southern regions of China.
  • 台郡 - Refers to the administrative region of Taiwan.
  • 海外劳吏 - Refers to officials working in overseas territories, emphasizing the administrative status of Taiwan.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 政令初施，人心未洽 - When new policies are first implemented, the people's hearts are not yet aligned.
  • 转眄易之 - To change one's mind quickly or to be fickle.
  • 萧规曹随 - To follow the rules set by predecessors without making changes.
  • 不事更张 - Not to make any changes.
  • 席不暇暖 - To be so busy that one does not have time to sit down and warm oneself.
  • 视一官传舍 - To regard an official position as a temporary lodging.
  • 孰肯为远效难稽之治乎 - Who would be willing to undertake a distant and difficult governance?
  • 欲化番人 - To civilize or transform the barbarians.
  • 分封 - Feudal enfeoffment.
  • 同姓及世卿采地 - Clan members and hereditary nobles with fiefs.
  • 子孙世守 - Descendants to inherit and protect.
  • 久任数十年 - To hold a position for many years.
  • 不责旦暮之效 - Not to demand immediate results.
  • 暗阻潜挠于中者 - Those who secretly obstruct and undermine from within.
  • 社棍是也 - These are the village bullies.
  • 内地犯法奸民 - Lawbreakers from the interior regions.
  • 逃死匿身于僻远无人之地 - To escape death and hide in remote and uninhabited places.
  • 谋充夥长通事 - To plot to become leaders or interpreters.
  • 为日既久，熟识番情 - Having been there for a long time, they are well acquainted with the customs of the barbarians.
  • 复解番语 - And understand the language of the barbarians.
  • 父死子继 - Fathers die and sons take over.
  • 流毒无已 - Their harmful influence continues endlessly.
  • 社商者 - The local officials.
  • 高卧郡邑，催饷纳课而已 - To sit idly in the county, collecting taxes and duties.
  • 社事任其播弄 - Leaving the affairs of the community to be manipulated by others.
  • 社商有亏折耗费 - When the local officials suffer losses or waste.
  • 此辈坐享其利 - These people enjoy the benefits.
  • 社商率一二岁更易 - Local officials are replaced every one or two years.
  • 而此辈虽死不移也 - But these people remain unchanged even if they die.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 萧规曹随 - Refers to the historical practice of following the rules and norms established by previous rulers, particularly during the Han Dynasty.
  • 番人 - Refers to non-Han Chinese groups, often used in a derogatory sense to describe ethnic minorities.
  • 分封 - Refers to the feudal system of land grants and titles, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 世卿采地 - Refers to hereditary nobility and their fiefs, a system where land and titles were passed down through generations.
  • 社商 - Refers to local officials or administrators, often responsible for managing the affairs of a community.
  • 社棍 - Refers to village bullies or local troublemakers, often associated with corruption and exploitation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此辈正利番人之愚 - These people take advantage of the ignorance of the barbarians. '利' means 'to take advantage of' or 'to benefit from,' and '愚' refers to 'ignorance' or 'stupidity.'
  • 撄夺惟意 - To seize or snatch at will. '撄夺' can be translated as 'seize' or 'snatch,' and '惟意' means 'at will' or 'as one pleases.'
  • 迫挟 - To coerce or to intimidate.
  • 听讼者仍问之通事 - The judge still asks the interpreter. '听讼者' refers to 'the judge' or 'the one who listens to lawsuits,' and '通事' means 'interpreter' or 'translator.'
  • 颠倒是非以对 - To deliberately reverse right and wrong in response. '颠倒' means 'to reverse' or 'to invert,' '是非' means 'right and wrong,' and '以对' means 'in response' or 'to answer.'
  • 番人反受呵责 - The barbarians are instead rebuked. '反' means 'instead' or 'on the contrary,' and '呵责' means 'rebuke' or 'scold.'
  • 县官以尔违通事夥长言，故怒责尔 - The county official is angry with you because you disobeyed what the interpreter and the headman said. '县官' means 'county official,' '违' means 'to disobey,' '夥长' means 'headman' or 'leader,' and '怒责' means 'to be angry and rebuke.'
  • 事之不啻帝天 - They treat them no less than gods. '事之' means 'to treat them,' '不啻' means 'no less than,' and '帝天' means 'gods' or 'heavenly beings.'
  • 情至于无告 - Their plight has reached a point of no recourse. '情' means 'plight' or 'situation,' '至于' means 'has reached,' and '无告' means 'point of no recourse' or 'helpless.'
  • 上之人无由知 - Those in high places have no way of knowing. '上之人' means 'those in high places' or 'the authorities,' and '无由知' means 'have no way of knowing.'
  • 举世所当哀矜者，莫番人若矣 - There is no one in the world more deserving of pity than the barbarians. '举世' means 'all over the world,' '所当哀矜者' means 'those who deserve pity,' and '莫番人若矣' means 'no one more so than the barbarians.'
  • 以其异类，且歧视之 - Because they are a different race, they are discriminated against. '以其' means 'because of,' '异类' means 'different race' or 'outsiders,' '且' means 'and,' and '歧视之' means 'they are discriminated against.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 番人 - Refers to 'barbarians' or 'foreigners,' often used in a derogatory sense to describe non-Han Chinese people. It reflects the ethnocentric view of the time.
  • 通事 - Refers to an 'interpreter' or 'translator,' typically used to mediate between officials and foreigners or people of different ethnic groups.
  • 夥长 - Refers to a 'headman' or 'leader' within a community, often a local authority figure.
  • 县官 - Refers to a 'county official' or 'local government official,' representing the authority of the central government at the local level.
  • 帝天 - Refers to 'gods' or 'heavenly beings,' often used in a religious or superstitious context.
  • 异类 - Means 'different race' or 'outsiders,' reflecting the social and cultural divide between Han Chinese and other ethnic groups.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 噫！ - Interjection expressing surprise, frustration, or disappointment.
  • 若亦人也，其肢体皮骨，何莫非人！ - Rhetorical question emphasizing the humanity of the subject, suggesting that if someone has human limbs and bones, they are indeed human.
  • 而云若是乎！ - Rhetorical question expressing disbelief or indignation at the situation described.
  • 马不宿驰，牛无偏驾，否且致疾。 - Metaphorical statement suggesting that if horses and oxen are overworked, they will become ill. This is used to argue against overworking humans.
  • 牛马且然，而况人乎！ - Rhetorical question emphasizing that if even animals suffer from overwork, how much more so would humans.
  • 抑知彼苟多帛，亦重绨矣！ - Rhetorical question suggesting that if someone has many fine silks, they would also value coarser fabrics, implying a sense of balance and moderation.
  • 寒胡为哉？ - Rhetorical question asking why someone would endure cold when they have the means to avoid it.
  • 彼苟无事，亦安居矣！ - Rhetorical question suggesting that if someone has no responsibilities, they should be able to live comfortably.
  • 暴露胡为哉？ - Rhetorical question asking why someone would expose themselves to harsh conditions when they have the means to avoid it.
  • 彼苟免力役，亦暇且逸矣！ - Rhetorical question suggesting that if someone is free from labor, they should have leisure and comfort.
  • 奔走负戴于社棍之室胡为哉？ - Rhetorical question asking why someone would run errands for local bullies when they could avoid it.
  • 乐饱暖而苦饥寒，厌劳役而安逸豫，人之性也，异其人，何必异其性哉？ - Statement asserting that people naturally prefer comfort and ease over hardship and labor, and that this is a universal human trait regardless of individual differences.
  • 郁君之言如此。 - Statement indicating that the above sentiments are those expressed by Mr. Yu.
  • 予昔闻闽人言，番民性宜率，最易感化，大可为用。 - Statement recalling a past conversation where someone from Fujian mentioned that the people of a certain region (likely referring to a minority group) are straightforward and easily influenced, making them valuable.
  • 况番地极暖，岁谷可三熟，且土壤膏腴，旷地最多。 - Statement describing the warm climate, fertile soil, and abundant land in the region.
  • 前山东刘玉坡制军韵珂总督浙、闽时，番民叩辕，呈请发帑入境开垦，计岁入赋税甚巨。 - Historical reference to a time when the governor of Shandong, Liu Yubo, was also in charge of Zhejiang and Fujian, and the local people petitioned for funds to cultivate the land, expecting significant tax revenue.
  • 制军大悦，特疏入告，惜为长白某相国所持，其事遂寝。 - Statement explaining that the governor was pleased and submitted a memorial, but the plan was ultimately rejected by a high-ranking official from Changbai.
  • 今证以郁君所记，果信。 - Statement affirming that the account provided by Mr. Yu confirms the earlier observations.
  • 域中有此沃壤，摈弃诚属可惜，爰附识于此，以俟当道之采择焉。 - Concluding statement expressing regret at the waste of fertile land and suggesting that the information be recorded for future consideration by those in power.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 闽人 - Refers to people from Fujian province.
  • 番民 - Refers to a minority group, possibly indigenous or non-Han Chinese people.
  • 番地 - Refers to the land inhabited by the minority group.
  • 山东刘玉坡制军韵珂 - Refers to Liu Yubo, a historical figure who served as a military governor in Shandong and later as a governor of Zhejiang and Fujian.
  • 长白某相国 - Refers to a high-ranking official from Changbai, likely a powerful figure who opposed the land development plan.

  • 疏入告 - Refers to the act of submitting a memorial or official document to the emperor.
  • 当道 - Refers to those in power or officials in charge of decision-making.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天无时无云 - Literal phrase meaning 'the sky never lacks clouds,' used to describe a perpetually cloudy sky.
  • 浓云叆叇 - Poetic phrase meaning 'thick, heavy clouds,' often used to describe a dense, oppressive cloud cover.
  • 云脚 - Refers to the lower edge of a cloud, where it meets the horizon or the ground.
  • 画家绘水口石 - Metaphor comparing the lower edge of a cloud to a painter's brushstroke that indicates the boundary between water and stone.
  • 日色被体如灼 - Vivid expression meaning 'the sun's rays feel like burning on the body,' describing intense sunlight.
  • 日出时 - Refers to the time of sunrise.
  • 辰刻后 - Refers to the hour of辰 (around 7 AM to 9 AM in the Chinese time system).
  • 日初出即开朗 - Phrase meaning 'the sun rises and immediately clears the sky,' indicating a clear morning.
  • 暑月久晴 - Refers to a prolonged period of clear weather during the hot summer months.
  • 日落时 - Refers to the time of sunset.
  • 西方有云气横亘天上 - Phrase meaning 'there are clouds stretching horizontally across the western sky.'
  • 数十缕 - Refers to 'several strands' or 'several threads.'
  • 各不相属 - Means 'not connected to each other.'
  • 日从云隙中渡过 - Vivid expression meaning 'the sun passes through the gaps in the clouds,' describing a clear sky with intermittent cloud cover.
  • 日穿经纬 - Poetic phrase meaning 'the sun passing through the weave of the sky,' often used to describe a clear sky with a few clouds.
  • 来日大晴 - Phrase meaning 'it will be clear the next day.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 海上占晴雨 - Refers to the practice of predicting weather changes based on observations of the sky at sea.
  • 无脚之云 - Metaphor for clouds that do not touch the ground, often used to describe high-altitude clouds.
  • 画远山 - Metaphor for clouds that are distant and not clearly defined, often used to describe high-altitude or wispy clouds.
  • 日色被体如灼 - Vivid expression that emphasizes the intensity of sunlight, often used in classical Chinese poetry to describe hot summer days.
  • 日穿经纬 - Poetic phrase that describes a clear sky with a few clouds, often used in classical Chinese literature to predict good weather.

  • 天无时无云，虽浓云叆叇，但有云脚可见，必不雨
  • 无脚之云，如画远山，但见山头，不见所止
  • 日色被体如灼，三日内必雨

  • 敢为缓颊 - I dare to intercede on his behalf; shows Shi Dakai's willingness to help.
