Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里乘子曰 - Conventional opening formula meaning 'Lǐchéngzi said,' introducing the author's commentary.

  • 金田啸聚 - Refers to the initial gathering of the Taiping forces in Jintian, Guangxi.
  • 党羽不过三百馀人 - Means 'the number of followers was no more than several hundred,' indicating the small size of the initial group.
  • 地方有司 - Refers to local officials or magistrates.

  • 格其邪心 - Means 'to correct their misguided thoughts,' implying the need to change their beliefs.
  • 执迷不悟 - Means 'to stubbornly refuse to understand,' describing those who persist in error.
  • 乘其势尚未张 - Means 'to take advantage of the situation before it becomes powerful,' suggesting early intervention.

  • 消患未形 - Means 'to eliminate the problem before it manifests,' suggesting preventive action.
  • 去莠安良 - Means 'to remove weeds and protect the good,' a metaphor for eliminating evil and preserving the virtuous.
  • 贤有司之责也 - Means 'the duty of capable officials,' emphasizing the responsibility of good governance.
  • 星星曤火 - A metaphor for small, scattered fires, symbolizing the initial stages of the rebellion.
  • 燎及昆冈 - Means 'to burn down the great mountains,' symbolizing the spread and intensification of the rebellion.
  • 涓涓氿泉 - A metaphor for small, trickling streams, symbolizing the early stages of the rebellion.
  • 荡成巨浸 - Means 'to become a vast flood,' symbolizing the rapid growth and spread of the rebellion.
  • 顺流下窜 - Means 'to flow downstream and scatter,' describing the movement of the rebels.
  • 窃据金陵 - Means 'to secretly occupy Jinling,' referring to the Taiping's capture of Nanjing.
  • 十馀年来 - Means 'for more than ten years,' indicating the duration of the conflict.
  • 沿江一带 - Means 'along the river region,' referring to the areas affected by the rebellion.
  • 民无完居 - Means 'people have no safe homes,' describing the destruction and displacement caused by the rebellion.
  • 屠毒生灵不可数计 - Means 'the slaughter of innocent lives is beyond count,' emphasizing the scale of the suffering.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 粤寇之变 - Refers to the Taiping Rebellion (1850-1864), a massive civil war in southern China led by Hong Xiuquan.
  • 金田啸聚 - Refers to the initial gathering of the Taiping forces in Jintian, Guangxi, which marked the beginning of the rebellion.
  • 金陵 - Refers to Nanjing, a significant city during the Taiping Rebellion, where the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom was established.
  • 沿江一带 - Refers to the Yangtze River region, which was heavily affected by the rebellion.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '奈苦无兵械，又不得当' is a compound sentence indicating the speaker's frustration over the lack of military resources and their inability to act effectively.
  • The phrase '藏有砒屑少许，为他日自尽地' is a complex sentence indicating that the speaker has hidden a small amount of arsenic for potential self-destruction.
  • The phrase '适见面心动，因潜下之，将图一快意' is a series of actions indicating that the speaker was moved by what they saw and decided to take the arsenic to satisfy their desire.
  • The phrase '虽粉骨亦甘心焉' is a conditional sentence indicating that the speaker is willing to die a painful death.
  • The phrase '今计不成，数也' is a conclusion indicating that the failure of the plan is due to fate.
  • The phrase '观贼所为必膺天诛，特时未至耳' is a conditional sentence indicating that the bandits' actions will certainly be punished by heaven, but the time has not yet come.
  • The phrase '我死固不足惜，独怪尔曹俨然须眉男子' is a contrast indicating that the speaker's death is not something to regret, but they are surprised by the behavior of the men.
  • The phrase '孰非食毛践土，竟腆颜事贼' is a rhetorical question indicating that everyone benefits from the land, yet the men shamelessly serve the bandits.
  • The phrase '为之爪牙，受其豕畜而犬嗾之' is a series of actions indicating that the men are the bandits' pawns and are treated like animals.
  • The phrase '一旦天兵云集，丑类尽歼，漏网难期，噬脐莫及' is a series of actions and conditions indicating that the imperial forces will suddenly gather, all the villains will be destroyed, escaping punishment is difficult, and it is too late to regret.

  • 吾独怪与秦小罗生同里闬 - I find it particularly strange or am especially puzzled by living in the same neighborhood or being from the same area as Qin Xiaoluosheng.
  • 乃一则以图贼而甘蹈于死，一则以媚贼而苦求其生 - Contrasting expressions meaning 'one on the one hand' and 'the other on the other hand'; plotting against bandits or fighting against criminals while willingly facing death, flattering bandits or criminals while desperately seeking to survive.
  • 迹其两人行事，一薰一莸，真判若天渊矣 - Tracing the actions of these two people, one fragrant, one foul, truly as different as heaven and earth or the sky and the abyss.
  • 试为顾名思义 - Let us try or let us consider the meaning of the name or understand the meaning by its name.
  • 小罗固不难继美于罗敷 - It is not difficult or it is certainly not hard to follow in the footsteps of Luo Fu, a famous virtuous woman in Chinese literature.
  • 若善祥者，则无善可称，不祥莫大焉 - If it comes to Shanxiang, there is nothing good to say about her, the greatest misfortune or the most unfortunate outcome.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 里乘子 - Likely a pseudonym or pen name of a classical Chinese writer or commentator.
  • 善祥 - A proper noun, likely referring to a historical or fictional woman named Shanxiang.
  • 秦小罗生 - A proper noun, likely referring to a historical or fictional woman named Qin Xiaoluosheng.
  • 罗敷 - Refers to Luo Fu, a virtuous and beautiful woman from the Book of Songs, often used as a model of feminine virtue.
  • 一薰一莸 - A metaphor from the Book of Songs meaning 'one fragrant, one foul' or 'one virtuous, one wicked,' often used to describe moral opposites.

  • 秦小罗生 - The name of the woman Qin Xiaoluosheng.
  • 罗敷 - The name of Luo Fu, a famous virtuous woman in Chinese literature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 只我有二子，其余三人为我伯叔行，齿皆逾六旬 - Only I have two sons, while the other three are my older brothers and uncles, all over sixty years old.
  • 景迫桑榆 - Metaphor for the decline of old age, derived from the phrase '桑榆之景' (the setting sun in the mulberry and elm trees), symbolizing the end of one's life.
  • 频年叠接家书，促我遄归，委以后事 - Receiving family letters frequently over the years, urging me to return quickly, and entrusting me with after matters.
  • 自我至中国贸易，今将三十年，已一世矣 - Since I came to China for trade, now nearly thirty years, already a lifetime.
  • 所获利约计三千万金有奇 - Approximately three million and some gold, indicating a large sum of wealth.
  • 屡欲赋归，以行务无人可托，故迟迟未决 - Often wanted to return home, but because there is no one to entrust the business to, thus delaying the decision.
  • 今幸得君，敢烦作替，未知君能许我乎？” - Now fortunately I have you, dare to trouble you to take my place, do not know if you can grant me this.
  • 易蹙然对曰 - Describes a worried or hesitant expression.
  • 年来谬承厚爱，视同骨肉 - Misleadingly receiving great affection, treating as flesh and blood, indicating a close familial relationship.
  • 如能效力，断不敢辞 - If I can be of service, certainly would not decline, indicating firm commitment.
  • 窃思行务非同细事，自顾年少材轻，深恐难肩重任 - Secretly think, business affairs are not trivial matters, looking at myself, young and lacking ability, deeply fear being unable to bear heavy responsibilities.
  • 虽君有命，不敢与闻 - Even if you command, would not dare to participate, indicating reluctance to take on the task.
  • 我筹之烂熟，目前克肩此任，舍君更无其人 - Have thought it through thoroughly, currently can shoulder this responsibility, without you, there is no one else.
  • 且与君约：我此次归去，重来与否不能预定，如三年不来，君即为此行主人，所有货物资本，概以相赠，一切君自主持，无庸迟回疑虑 - And I make an agreement with you: this time I will return, but whether I come back again cannot be predetermined, if I do not come back within three years, you will be the owner of this business, all goods and capital will be given to you, you will take full charge of everything, no need to hesitate or doubt.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伯叔行 - Refers to older brothers and uncles, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 桑榆 - Metaphor for the decline of old age, derived from the phrase '桑榆之景' (the setting sun in the mulberry and elm trees), symbolizing the end of one's life.
  • 厚资 - Refers to substantial wealth or assets, indicating the family's financial status.
  • 嗣息 - Refers to descendants or heirs, indicating the family's concern about inheritance.
  • 赋归 - Refers to retiring or returning home, indicating the speaker's desire to end his business career.
  • 行务 - Refers to business affairs or responsibilities, indicating the family's concerns about managing the business.
  • 货物资本 - Refers to goods and capital, indicating the family's assets.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence introduces the speaker's family situation, emphasizing the uniqueness of having two sons compared to the older brothers and uncles who are all over sixty.
  • This clause describes the family's financial status and the lack of heirs, indicating their concern about inheritance.
  • This clause explains the family's ongoing communication and their urging for the speaker to return home and take responsibility for after matters.
  • This clause provides background on the speaker's long career in trade, emphasizing the duration of his business activities.
  • This clause highlights the speaker's significant wealth, indicating the success of his business.
  • This clause explains the speaker's repeated desires to retire but his inability to do so due to the lack of a successor.
  • This clause introduces the speaker's request for the listener to take over the business, indicating his hope and uncertainty.
  • This clause describes the listener's hesitant response, indicating their concern.
  • This clause expresses the listener's gratitude and close relationship with the speaker.
  • This clause indicates the listener's willingness to help, emphasizing their commitment.
  • This clause expresses the listener's concern about their ability to handle the business, indicating self-doubt.
  • This clause indicates the listener's reluctance to take on the task, emphasizing their hesitation.
  • This clause indicates the listener's confidence in the speaker's ability and the uniqueness of the speaker.
  • This clause indicates the formal promise and the conditional transfer of responsibility and assets.

  • 武斷 - Literally 'to decide by force,' but here it means 'domineering' or 'arbitrary.'

  • 通家 - Refers to families that have close ties or intermarriage.
  • 端姑發才覆額 - Means 'Duan Gu's hair just covers her forehead,' describing her young age and appearance.
  • 女紅之暇且粗識字 - Means 'in her spare time from needlework, she learned to read and write a little,' indicating her modest education.

  • 死守非計 - Means 'holding on rigidly is not a good plan,' suggesting that clinging to a situation is not wise.
  • 擇人改字為善 - Means 'it would be better to choose another person for marriage,' implying a change in marital plans.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 江西 - Refers to Jiangxi Province, a region in southeastern China.

  • 監司 - A high-ranking official in the Qing Dynasty, responsible for overseeing regional administration.

  • 擇人改字 - Refers to the practice of changing a marriage alliance, often due to unforeseen circumstances.

  • 且系妇翁 - Phrase meaning 'and he was related to his mother-in-law', indicating a familial relationship.
  • 暂往相依 - Phrase meaning 'temporarily go to rely on him', suggesting a temporary stay.

  • 夫妻望眼欲穿 - Phrase meaning 'my wife and I were on the verge of despair', expressing deep concern.

  • 长郎又泣告所以 - Phrase meaning 'the eldest son wept and told the reason', describing a narrative.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 他日倘踪迹而至 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if one day they trace their steps and arrive.'

  • 刘大喜，乃欢笑订盟而别 - Phrase meaning 'Liu was greatly pleased and they happily sealed the alliance before parting.'
  • 翌日，刘遣冰纳采委禽 - Phrase meaning 'the next day, Liu sent an ice messenger to present gifts and accept the betrothal offerings.'

  • 涓吉亲迎 - Phrase meaning 'chose a favorable date for the wedding.'

  • 甲固有河东之俱 - Phrase meaning 'Jia indeed has a connection to Hedong.'
  • 欲藉刘横暴以制服其妻 - Phrase meaning 'wants to use Liu's violent nature to control his wife.'
  • 既订盟，归以告妻，妻怒不答 - Phrase meaning 'after sealing the alliance, he returned home to tell his wife, but she angrily refused to respond.'
  • 及将届吉期，又与妻谋曰 - Phrase meaning 'when the wedding date was approaching, he again plotted with his wife saying.'
  • 端儿于归在迩 - Phrase meaning 'Duan's wedding is near.'
  • 留长郎在，终是未断葛藤 - Phrase meaning 'leaving Changlang here will never sever the ties.'
  • 吾今夜决意手刃之，以杜后患 - Phrase meaning 'I have decided tonight to kill him to eliminate future troubles.'
  • 妻闻言，戟手唾面骂曰 - Phrase meaning 'upon hearing this, the wife raised her fist and spat in his face, cursing him.'
  • 以若所为，天良丧尽，狗彘不食其余 - Phrase meaning 'because of what you have done, your conscience is completely lost; even dogs and pigs would not eat your leftovers.'

  • 古称六州铁不能铸一大错 - Proverb meaning 'as the saying goes, six states' iron cannot forge one great mistake.'
  • 我今未尝不悔 - Phrase meaning 'I have never regretted this.'
  • 刘某横暴，卿所知也 - Phrase meaning 'Liu's violent nature is something you know well.'
  • 倘有反覆，彼甘心耶 - Phrase meaning 'if there is any change, will he be satisfied?'
  • 今我所谓势同骑虎 - Metaphor for a dangerous situation where one cannot easily back out.
  • 卿纵不忍故婿，独不为夫计乎 - Phrase meaning 'even if you cannot bear to harm your former son-in-law, don't you think about your husband's situation?'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 長郎正挑燈觀書 - Literally 'the young master was holding a lamp and reading a book,' a common idiom in Classical Chinese to describe someone studying late at night.

  • 大驚，叱曰 - 'Greatly surprised, he shouted,' a common expression for reacting with shock and rebuke.
  • 暮夜卿何至此 - 'Why are you here at night?' a direct and somewhat abrupt question.
  • 與卿名為夫婦，尚未成禮 - 'Although we are named husband and wife, the rites have not been completed,' a formal way of acknowledging an incomplete marriage.
  • 卿父視我猶子 - 'Your father regards me as his son,' a respectful way of expressing a close familial relationship.
  • 是禽獸也 - 'That would make me a beast,' a strong condemnation of immoral behavior.
  • 瓜李之嫌 - Refers to the 'Gua-Li' incident, a historical event where a man was accused of impropriety with a woman, symbolizing suspicion or scandal.
  • 卿宜速退 - 'You should leave quickly,' a polite but firm command.
  • 噫！妾豈淫奔者耶 - 'Ah! Am I a woman who runs away with a man?' a rhetorical question expressing indignation.
  • 君禍且不測 - 'Your misfortune is unpredictable,' indicating severe and uncertain danger.
  • 宜速為計 - 'You should act quickly,' urging immediate action.
  • 勿迂守頭巾氣也 - 'Do not stubbornly cling to formalities,' advising practicality over rigid adherence to rules.
  • 長郎聞之失色 - 'The young master was shocked and lost color,' describing a strong emotional reaction.
  • 長跪求救 - 'He knelt long to beg for help,' a sign of deep respect and desperation.
  • 事急矣！計惟从郎偕亡 - 'The situation is urgent! The only plan is to die with you,' expressing a dire and desperate situation.
  • 由后门出，再作别议 - 'We will leave through the back door and discuss other matters later,' a practical and discreet plan.
  • 端姑约略记其大概 - 'Duanggu vaguely remembered the general location,' indicating partial recall.
  • 揣度形势 - 'Assessing the situation,' a common phrase for evaluating circumstances.
  • 彳亍前行 - 'Walking slowly and hesitantly,' describing cautious movement.
  • 昧爽 - 'Dawn,' a poetic term for early morning.
  • 踉跄犯露晓行 - 'Stumbling and hurriedly walking in the morning dew,' describing a rushed and disheveled appearance.
  • 形迹可疑 - 'Suspicious behavior,' indicating unusual or questionable actions.
  • 要至室中 - 'He brought them into the room,' a common phrase for inviting someone inside.
  • 研悉巅末 - 'Investigating the details,' a formal way of thoroughly examining a situation.
  • 大惊 - 'Greatly surprised,' a strong emotional reaction.
  • 见二人暮夜奔驰，气急败坏 - 'Seeing two people rushing at night, their breaths ragged and disheveled,' describing a chaotic and urgent scene.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以袖拂面谛视之 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'wiping the face with a sleeve and looking carefully at it,' used to depict a detailed inspection.
  • 女固娟好，男亦婉娈 - '固' means 'indeed' or 'certainly,' '娟好' means 'graceful and beautiful,' and '婉娈' means 'graceful and charming.' This phrase describes the beauty of both the girl and the boy.
  • 大喜，慰令毋恐 - '大喜' means 'greatly delighted,' and '慰令毋恐' means 'comforting them not to be afraid.'
  • 遂并纳而藏之 - '遂' means 'then' or 'thereupon,' '并纳' means 'together accepted,' and '藏之' means 'and hid them.' This phrase describes the action of accepting and hiding both individuals.
  • 遍戒众人，秘勿妄语 - '遍戒' means 'to warn everyone,' '秘' means 'secretly,' and '勿妄语' means 'not to speak falsely.' This phrase describes the instruction to keep the matter secret.
  • 众窥刘意，誓不敢泄 - '众窥刘意' means 'the crowd understood Liu's intention,' and '誓不敢泄' means 'swore not to leak it.' This phrase describes the crowd's commitment to secrecy.
  • 又窃叹二人何其命蹇 - '又窃叹' means 'also secretly sighed,' and '何其命蹇' means 'how unfortunate their fate.' This phrase expresses sympathy for the unfortunate fate of the two individuals.
  • 虎口方离，旋投陷井 - '虎口方离' means 'just escaped from the tiger's mouth,' and '旋投陷井' means 'immediately fell into a trap.' This phrase describes the sudden change in their situation.
  • 自贻伊戚，夫复何尤 - '自贻伊戚' means 'self-inflicted sorrow,' and '夫复何尤' means 'what blame can there be?' This phrase reflects on the inevitability of their fate.
  • 盖甲妹与刘某家同在甲家东偏 - '盖' means 'it is said that' or 'it is known that,' '甲妹' means 'Jia's sister,' and '东偏' means 'eastern side.' This phrase provides context about the locations of Jia's sister and Liu's family.
  • 出门即是歧路 - '出门即是' means 'as soon as they went out,' and '歧路' means 'forked road.' This phrase describes the immediate encounter with a fork in the road.
  • 本应下达，反趋上游 - '本应' means 'should have,' '下达' means 'descended,' and '反趋' means 'instead went toward,' '上游' means 'upstream.' This phrase explains the mistake in direction.
  • 以致舛错 - '以致' means 'leading to,' and '舛错' means 'mistake' or 'error.' This phrase indicates the result of the mistake.
  • 其夜，甲见责于妻 - '其夜' means 'that night,' '见责于妻' means 'was scolded by his wife.' This phrase describes Jia being scolded by his wife.
  • 自知理绌 - '自知' means 'realized,' and '理绌' means 'lack of reason.' This phrase describes Jia's realization of his lack of justification.
  • 以妻恨恨而卧 - '以' means 'because,' '恨恨' means 'angry and resentful,' and '而卧' means 'lying down.' This phrase describes Jia's wife's anger.
  • 伺其熟睡，漏已三下 - '伺' means 'waited until,' '熟睡' means 'deep sleep,' and '漏已三下' means 'the water clock had struck three times.' This phrase describes Jia waiting until his wife was asleep.
  • 乃袖刃疾至书斋 - '乃' means 'then,' '袖刃' means 'with a knife hidden in his sleeve,' and '疾至' means 'hurriedly went to.' This phrase describes Jia going to the study with a hidden knife.
  • 索长郎不得，大惊 - '索' means 'searched for,' '长郎' means 'the eldest son,' and '不得' means 'could not find,' '大惊' means 'greatly alarmed.' This phrase describes Jia's alarm upon not finding the eldest son.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 官 - Refers to a magistrate or local official.
  • 伍伯 - Refers to constables or officers responsible for law enforcement.
  • 孀妹 - Refers to a widowed sister-in-law.
  • 雉经 - An ancient method of suicide by hanging.
  • 合卺 - A traditional Chinese wedding ceremony where the bride and groom share a wine cup from a gourd to symbolize their union.
  • 横暴 - Refers to someone who is violent and domineering.
  • 武断一乡 - Refers to someone who is authoritarian and makes decisions without considering others' opinions.

Proper Nouns

  • 官 - The magistrate.
  • 刘 - The person named Liu.
  • 甲 - The person named Jia.
  • 长郎 - Jia's son.
  • 端姑 - Jia's daughter.

  • 诘朝 - The next day, a formal way to refer to the following day.
  • 送归旧夫 - Send her back to her former husband, referring to the act of returning a woman to her previous marital partner.
  • 价值百缗并以奉贶 - The value of a hundred strings of cash, and he presented it as a gift, indicating a monetary transaction and a gesture of goodwill.
  • 收泪叩谢 - She wiped away her tears and bowed to thank him, describing a physical and emotional response to gratitude.
  • 权子母 - To engage in usury, a term used to describe lending money at interest.
  • 终岁，小有经营，获利倍蓰 - At the end of the year, with a little effort in business, he made a profit several times over, indicating a successful business venture.
  • 分半利往酬贾 - To share half of the profit to repay the merchant, showing a gesture of repayment.
  • 善生财者 - One who is good at generating wealth, praising someone's financial acumen.
  • 资本太少 - The capital is too small, indicating a limitation in resources.
  • 假子二千缗 - Lend me two thousand strings of cash, asking for a loan.
  • 戋戋半利 - A small amount of half the profit, referring to a modest sum.
  • 俟岁星一周 - Wait until the year has passed, using a celestial metaphor to indicate a period of time.
  • 好音 - Good news, a euphemism for positive information.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 媒人 - Matchmaker, a figure in traditional Chinese society who arranges marriages.
  • 旧夫 - Former husband, a term used in the context of a woman's previous marriage.
  • 长沙 - Changsha, a city in Hunan Province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 缗 - A unit of currency, typically a string of coins, used in ancient China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 媒人误我！媒人误我！This repetition emphasizes the speaker's frustration and disbelief.
  • 微娘子言，几陷我于不义矣The conditional clause highlights the near miss of a moral or legal transgression.
  • 遂慰令勿哀，诘朝仍送归旧夫The sequence of actions shows the immediate response to the situation and the subsequent resolution.
  • 价值百缗并以奉贶The phrase combines a monetary value with a gesture of goodwill, emphasizing both the financial and emotional aspects.
  • 某氏闻之，收泪叩谢The sequence of actions shows the emotional and physical response of the woman.
  • 贾召厨媪来伴某氏，自秉烛独宿外室The actions describe the merchant's care for the woman and his own actions.
  • 天明，令媒召某甲至，告以意，嘱携妻归The sequence of actions shows the merchant's instructions and the response of the man.
  • 某氏谓甲曰：“妾闻受恩必报。”The dialogue highlights the woman's sense of obligation and gratitude.
  • 甲点首曰：“卿言是也。”The dialogue shows the man's agreement and commitment.
  • 终岁，小有经营，获利倍蓰The sequence describes the man's business activities and their results.
  • 分半利往酬贾The action shows the man's gesture of repayment.
  • 贾喜曰：“子真可谓善生财者也。”The dialogue highlights the merchant's praise and further assistance.
  • 假子二千缗，假子二千缗The repetition emphasizes the merchant's generosity.
  • 俟岁星一周，当听君好音也The metaphorical phrase indicates a waiting period.
  • 携资如长沙The action describes the man's journey with financial resources.

Proper Nouns

  • 某贾 - A merchant, a generic term for a businessperson.
  • 某甲 - A man, a generic term for a male individual.
  • 某氏 - A woman, a generic term for a female individual.
  • 长沙 - Changsha, a city in Hunan Province.

[722]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以米价廉可获重息 - Means 'because the price of rice is cheap, it can yield high interest,' referring to the profit motive behind buying rice.
  • 爰买巨舟载米 - Means 'therefore, he bought a large ship to load rice,' indicating the action taken based on the previous reasoning.
  • 将行，会楚兵援皖 - Means 'just as he was about to depart, he encountered the Chu army coming to aid Anhui,' introducing an unexpected event.
  • 甲舟遂为群卒所据 - Means 'his ship was then seized by a group of soldiers,' describing the immediate consequence of the encounter.
  • 比至武昌，甲哀求偏将，愿出资买舟易换 - Means 'by the time he reached Wuchang, Jia, in desperation, asked a subordinate general to buy the ship and exchange it,' showing Jia's efforts to regain control of his ship.
  • 偏将见甲舟宽大，叱去群卒，自为坐船，甲无如何，只合听之 - Means 'the subordinate general, seeing Jia's ship was spacious, ordered the soldiers to leave and took it as his own. Jia had no choice but to accept it,' describing the subordinate general's actions and Jia's acceptance.
  • 偏将甚喜甲之为人，抵皖，寻拔皖城 - Means 'the subordinate general was very pleased with Jia's character and, upon reaching Anhui, soon captured the city,' indicating the general's positive reaction and subsequent success.
  • 各营需粮甚殷，偏将代甲分售，获利无算 - Means 'the various camps were in great need of food, so the subordinate general sold the rice on Jia's behalf and made a huge profit,' showing the general's business acumen.
  • 贼所掳两湖妇女甚多，甲求偏将，以百余缗谋得一妇人，年可三十许，且有一媪一童为伴，将归以报贾 - Means 'many women from the Two Lakes region were captured by the rebels. Jia asked the subordinate general to buy one for him with over a hundred strings of cash. The woman was about thirty years old and had an elderly woman and a child accompanying her. He intended to bring her back to repay Jia,' detailing the purchase and the composition of the group.
  • 既旋汉口，备奁择日，使妻送妇至贾家 - Means 'after returning to Hankou, he prepared a dowry, chose a date, and sent his wife to deliver the woman to Jia's home,' describing the preparations and the delivery.
  • 方下舆，妇与贾觌面，不禁相持恸哭失声 - Means 'as the woman stepped down from the carriage, she and Jia saw each other face to face and could not help but embrace and weep uncontrollably,' depicting the emotional reunion.
  • 妇人非他，即贾被掳之妻也 - Means 'the woman was none other than the wife Jia had been captured with,' revealing the identity of the woman.
  • 甲妻闻之，大喜称贺 - Means 'Jia's wife heard about it and was very happy and congratulated him,' showing the family's reaction.
  • 贾问妻，知老母、幼子俱无恙，益喜 - Means 'Jia asked his wife and learned that his elderly mother and young son were both safe. He was even more delighted,' indicating the further good news.
  • 其母与子非他，即妇人为伴之一媪一童也 - Means 'his mother and son were none other than the elderly woman and child who accompanied the woman,' revealing the identities of the additional individuals.
  • 贾亟命舆迎母与子归，一家团聚，悲喜并集，乃刑牲演剧，以报天佑 - Means 'Jia immediately ordered a carriage to bring his mother and son back. The family was reunited, and joy and sorrow mingled. They sacrificed animals and staged a play to thank heaven for their good fortune,' describing the family's celebration.
  • 自是贾与甲缔交，竟成通家，往来无间焉 - Means 'from then on, Jia and the subordinate general became close friends and eventually became like one family, visiting each other frequently,' showing the lasting friendship.
  • 刑牲演剧 - Refers to the traditional practice of sacrificing animals and staging plays to honor deities or celebrate significant events.
  • 天佑 - Refers to divine protection or favor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 楚兵援皖 - Refers to the Chu army coming to aid Anhui, a historical event.
  • 武昌 - Refers to Wuchang, a city in Hubei Province, known for its strategic importance.
  • 两湖 - Refers to the Two Lakes region, encompassing Hunan and Hubei provinces.
  • 汉口 - Refers to Hankou, a part of modern-day Wuhan, a major city in Hubei Province.

Proper Nouns

  • 楚兵 - The Chu army.
  • 武昌 - Wuchang.
  • 两湖 - The Two Lakes region.
  • 汉口 - Hankou.
  • 天佑 - Divine protection or favor.

[723]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里乘子曰 - Formal opening, '里乘子' is a title or name of the author or speaker, '曰' means 'says' or 'states'.
  • 贾还甲之妻 - Literally 'Jia returns Jia's wife', a play on words or metaphorical expression suggesting Jia is returning something of great value to Jia.
  • 甲亦还贾之妻 - Similarly, 'Jia also returns Jia's wife', reinforcing the idea of mutual return.
  • 不惟还其妻，且还其母、其子 - Means 'not only returns his wife, but also his mother and son', indicating a broader scope of return.
  • 贾之所获利益多矣 - Means 'Jia has gained much benefit', emphasizing the positive outcome.
  • 盖贾不忍使人骨肉分离 - Means 'because Jia could not bear to see his family separated', indicating Jia's emotional and moral stance.
  • 天亦断不忍使贾骨肉分离 - Means 'Heaven also could not bear to see Jia's family separated', suggesting divine intervention or alignment with Jia's actions.
  • 此中因缘凑合，造物煞费安排 - Means 'The coincidences and arrangements in this matter are truly intricate', highlighting the complexity and precision of the events.
  • 倘有毫厘之失，即不堪设想矣 - Means 'If there were even the slightest mistake, it would be unimaginable', emphasizing the critical nature of the situation.
  • 凡百君子，可不勉哉 - Means 'All gentlemen should strive to do so', serving as a moral exhortation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 里乘子 - The name or title of the author or speaker, likely a respected figure in the context of the text.
  • 贾 - A person's name, possibly a merchant or a person of significant social standing.
  • 甲 - Another person's name, likely a family member or a close associate of Jia.
  • 骨肉分离 - Refers to the separation of family members, a deeply emotional and culturally significant concept in Chinese society.
  • 造物 - Refers to the creator or the universe, often used in philosophical or religious contexts.
  • 凡百君子 - Refers to all virtuous and respected individuals, a common term in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 里乘子 - The name or title of the author or speaker.
  • 贾 - A person's name.
  • 甲 - Another person's name.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 全部累月，笙歌不绝 - Means performances lasting for months with continuous music and singing.
  • 士女云集，举国若狂 - Describes a scene where both men and women gather, and the entire country is in a frenzy.
  • 生意夺神驰，几难自主 - Indicates being so captivated that one almost loses control.
  • 预使仆侦探 - Means sending a servant to spy on or keep watch.
  • 遥而觑之，立不移步 - Describes looking at something from a distance without moving.

  • 知女名蕊霞 - Indicates learning the name of a girl named Rui Xia.
  • 父曾官游击 - Refers to her father having been an officer in the military.
  • 仅此一女，钟爱特甚 - Means she is the only daughter and was greatly loved by her father.
  • 幼延师课读 - Indicates being taught by a tutor from a young age.
  • 诗词歌赋，粗能脱稿 - Means being able to write poems, lyrics, songs, and essays, though not perfectly.
  • 亦使女习之 - Means teaching her these skills as well.

  • 宦橐萧条 - Refers to financial difficulties or poverty after the father's death.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生訪之既確 - Sheng visited and confirmed; '既確' emphasizes the thoroughness of the visit.
  • 演剧既毕 - The play has ended; '既毕' indicates the completion of an event.
  • 犹恋不忍舍 - Still loves and cannot bear to part; expresses strong attachment.
  • 日必一过女郎之门 - Every day, he must pass by the young lady's door; indicates a habitual action.
  • 无论见与不见，其心始慊 - Whether he sees her or not, his heart is only satisfied; '始慊' means 'only satisfied.'
  • 否则，忽忽若有所失 - Otherwise, he feels restless as if something is missing; describes a state of unease.
  • 门外固有蔷薇一架 - Outside the door, there is a trellis of roses; '固有' means 'there is indeed.'
  • 蕊霞适撷花簪髻 - Rui Xia is just picking flowers to adorn her hair; '适' indicates a timely or coincidental action.
  • 嫣然含睇，红涨于颊 - She looks charmingly at him, and her cheeks turn red; '嫣然' means 'charmingly' and '红涨于颊' describes blushing.
  • 生瞷无人，托言行路苦渴，长揖求饮 - Sheng, seeing no one, pretends to be thirsty on the road and bows to ask for a drink; '瞷' means 'to see secretly' and '托言' means 'to pretend.'
  • 蕊霞颔之，便呼婢取茶 - Rui Xia nods in agreement and calls a maid to fetch tea; '颔之' means 'nods in agreement.'
  • 生称娖向前，径诘氏族 - Sheng respectfully steps forward and directly asks about her family; '称娖' means 'respectfully' and '径诘' means 'directly asks.'
  • 女郎含笑具告之，亦转诘生氏族 - The young lady smiles and tells him, also asking about his family; '具告之' means 'tells him in detail.'
  • 彼此酬对，殷如熟识 - They exchange words, as if they are already familiar with each other; '殷如熟识' means 'as if they are already familiar.'
  • 须臾，婢捧茶至 - In a moment, the maid brings the tea; '须臾' means 'in a moment.'
  • 生饮毕兴辞 - Sheng drinks and then rises to take his leave; '兴辞' means 'rises to take leave.'
  • 妾知君为风雅士，母归，妾当相告。君得暇第至此，母爱才，必不见拒也。 - I know you are a man of culture; when my mother returns, I will tell her. If you have free time, please come here. My mother loves talent and will not refuse you.
  • 生闻之喜出望外，诺诺而别 - Sheng hears this and is overjoyed, nodding and taking his leave; '喜出望外' means 'overjoyed' and '诺诺' means 'nodding.'
  • 既归，忆女所言，以为有情，辗转沉思，彻夜不能交睫 - After returning, he recalls what the young lady said and thinks she has feelings for him, turning over in thought all night, unable to sleep; '辗转沉思' means 'turning over in thought' and '彻夜不能交睫' means 'unable to sleep all night.'
  • 翌日饭后，具冠服，执贽往拜其母 - The next day after breakfast, he dresses formally and carries a gift to pay respects to her mother; '具冠服' means 'dresses formally' and '执贽' means 'carries a gift.'
  • 母果出见，年四十以来，不失大家风范 - Her mother indeed comes out to see him, aged forty or more, and still maintains the grace of a noble lady; '不失大家风范' means 'still maintains the grace of a noble lady.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 感且不朽 - A literary expression meaning 'gratitude that endures forever,' emphasizing the lasting impact of the gesture.
  • 老妇昨适外出，致多简亵 - A humble expression where '简亵' (simplified and disrespectful) refers to the mother's attire or behavior, which she considers inappropriate.
  • 归闻小女道及郎君风雅 - '风雅' (elegant and refined) describes the young man's demeanor or character, as mentioned by the daughter.
  • 得暇第过我少坐，亦可藉辉蓬荜 - '得暇' (when you have time) and '第过我少坐' (please come by for a brief visit) are polite requests; '藉辉蓬荜' (to bring honor to my humble abode) indicates the mother's respect for the young man.
  • 生唯唯谦让 - '唯唯' (nodding in agreement) and '谦让' (modestly declining) describe the young man's polite response.
  • 自是靡日不往 - '靡日不往' (without fail, he visited every day) emphasizes the frequency of the visits.
  • 择婿但求快意，何必以远嫁为嫌 - '快意' (pleasure or satisfaction) and '远嫁为嫌' (objection to a distant marriage) express the uncle's perspective on marriage.
  • 儿女大事，何可草草 - '儿女大事' (marriage is a major matter) and '草草' (hastily) emphasize the importance of careful consideration.
  • 亡何，粤寇下窜，各道征兵孔急 - '亡何' (not long after) and '粤寇下窜' (the Cantonese bandits descended) describe the sudden outbreak of rebellion and the urgent call for troops.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 感且不朽 - Reflects the Confucian value of gratitude and the desire for lasting remembrance.
  • 老妇昨适外出，致多简亵 - Highlights the traditional modesty and self-deprecation of older women in classical Chinese culture.
  • 归闻小女道及郎君风雅 - Reflects the Confucian ideal of refinement and moral character.
  • 得暇第过我少坐，亦可藉辉蓬荜 - Demonstrates the importance of social etiquette and the humility of the host.
  • 生唯唯谦让 - Shows the young man's politeness and respect for social norms.
  • 自是靡日不往 - Indicates the close relationship and frequent visits between the young man and the family.
  • 蕊霞有舅，为邑诸生 - Reflects the importance of family connections and the status of local scholars.
  • 彼此倾谈，殊属投契 - Highlights the value of mutual understanding and rapport in social interactions.
  • 择婿但求快意，何必以远嫁为嫌 - Reflects the traditional view of marriage as a matter of personal satisfaction and family arrangement.
  • 儿女大事，何可草草 - Emphasizes the seriousness of marriage and the need for careful consideration.
  • 亡何，粤寇下窜，各道征兵孔急 - Refers to the historical context of the Taiping Rebellion (1850-1864), a major civil war in southern China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翠袖薄 - Describes thin green sleeves, symbolizing delicacy and fragility.

  • 颦眉默卷水晶帘 - Describes a woman frowning and silently rolling up a crystal curtain, conveying a sense of melancholy or introspection.

  • 吁嗟一更兮更鼓敲 - Lament or sigh, emphasizing the passage of time and the sound of the drum.
  • 蝴蝶同梦鸳鸯交 - Metaphor comparing the dream of a butterfly to the union of mandarin ducks, symbolizing fleeting or idealized love.
  • 二更嫦娥扶影立 - Refers to the second watch of the night, with the moon goddess Chang'e standing in her shadow, evoking a sense of solitude or longing.
  • 窃药缘何背后羿 - Reference to the myth of Chang'e stealing the elixir of immortality from Houyi, symbolizing forbidden love or regret.
  • 天上人间一片心 - Expresses a shared heart between heaven and earth, suggesting unity or longing across realms.
  • 细语花阴绣鞋湿 - Describes soft whispers in the shade of flowers, with embroidered shoes becoming wet, symbolizing emotional intensity or tears.

  • 强暴 - Refers to force or violence, often used in the context of military or criminal actions.
  • 复何能为 - A rhetorical question meaning 'what can they do again?' implying that the threat is not significant.
  • 敛重资而不肯募勇 - Means 'gathered heavy wealth but were unwilling to recruit brave men,' highlighting the contrast between wealth and action.
  • 斥其造言惑众 - Means 'scolded them for spreading false rumors to confuse the masses,' indicating a dismissive and harsh response.
  • 箝口而遁 - Means 'clamped their mouths shut and fled,' describing the spies' reaction to being punished.
  • 弃城而逃 - Means 'abandoned the city and fled,' indicating a panicked and cowardly action.
  • 屠戮殆尽 - Means 'almost completely slaughtered,' emphasizing the severity of the massacre.
  • 辇重资远避僻壤 - Means 'carried heavy wealth to hide in remote areas,' describing the action of the family members.
  • 穷搜得之 - Means 'searched thoroughly and found them,' indicating a determined effort to locate the family.
  • 杀劫淫虏 - Means 'killed, looted, raped, and abducted,' listing the various forms of violence and exploitation.
  • 一家靡有孑遗 - Means 'not a single member of the family survived,' emphasizing the complete destruction of the family.
  • 报应显然 - Means 'retribution is clear,' indicating a moral judgment.
  • 不惜出资募勇自卫 - Means 'spared no expense to recruit brave men for self-defense,' showing a proactive and protective attitude.
  • 受祸尤惨 - Means 'suffered a particularly cruel fate,' emphasizing the severity of the punishment.
  • 不犹彼胜于此欤 - A rhetorical question meaning 'isn't he better off than them?' suggesting a moral comparison.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 献贼 - Refers to Zhang Xianzhong, a rebel leader during the late Ming dynasty.
  • 粤寇 - Refers to the rebels from Guangdong (Yue), another group of rebels during the late Ming dynasty.
  • 方伯某公叔侄 - Refers to a specific official and his nephew, likely a historical figure, though the exact identity is not provided.
  • 某绅之子甲乙等 - Refers to the sons of a certain gentleman, named as A and B, who are unnamed but likely local figures.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 咸丰癸丑 - Refers to the year 1853 during the reign of Emperor Xianfeng of the Qing dynasty, a time of significant turmoil due to the Taiping Rebellion.
  • 粤寇 - Refers to the Taiping rebels, who were often called 'Yueguo' (Yue invaders) because they originated from Guangdong (Yue).
  • 贼蚁聚吾皖 - Refers to the Taiping forces gathering in Anhui (Wuan), a province in eastern China.
  • 棺木 - Refers to the coffin, a significant object in Chinese funerary practices.
  • 殡后 - Refers to the period after the funeral rites, a time when the deceased is considered to be in a state of transition.
  • 权厝 - Refers to a temporary burial, often used when a permanent burial site is not immediately available.
  • 大兵猝至 - Refers to the sudden arrival of Qing forces, which often resulted in the defeat of the Taiping rebels.
  • 棺之前和燃灼之迹尚存 - Refers to the partial success of the rebels' attempt to burn the coffin, a practice that was sometimes used to desecrate graves.

Proper Nouns

  • 里乘子 - A pseudonym or title, likely used by the author.
  • 咸丰 - The era name of Emperor Xianfeng of the Qing dynasty.
  • 粤寇 - The Taiping rebels.
  • 史太恭人 - The author's deceased mother, with '太恭人' being a posthumous title.
  • 滦州夜话 - A specific reference to a previous work or event, likely a literary or historical context.
